---
title: "Hemochromatosis model in FinnGen"
author: Jarkko Toivonen
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
execute:
  keep-md: true
format: 
  html:
    df-print: kable
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    embed-resources: true
  gfm: default
---

## NOTES

* HC follow-up is 1.5 years longer than donation follow-up (which is from R11)

## Setup

```{r setup}
# This is needed by tidymodels
#install.packages("/finngen/green/sfd_0.1.0.tar.gz")
#install.packages(c("tidyverse", "glue", "glmnet", "broom", "patchwork", "car", "bayesplot", "ggridges",
#                   "tictoc", "performance", "survminer", "tidymodels))
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(glmnet)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)
library(car)
# install.packages(c("/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/vip_0.4.1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/fmsb_0.7.5.tar.gz"),
#                    repos=NULL)
library(vip)
library(fmsb)
# These are needed for bayesian
# install.packages(c("shiny", "DT", "xts", "miniUI", "packrat", "RcppParallel", "StanHeaders", "inline", "loo",
#                    "BH", "rstan", "brms"))
# Maybe some of the below packages are also available from local CRAN. Check before running the command!
# install.packages(c("/finngen/green/nleqslv_3.3.5.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/Brobdingnag_1.2-6.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/bridgesampling_1.1-2.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/shinyjs_2.1.0.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/colourpicker_1.1.1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/dygraphs_1.1.1.6.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/threejs_0.3.3.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/rsconnect_0.8.25.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/shinythemes_1.2.0.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/rstantools_2.1.1.tar.gz",
#                    #"/finngen/green/rstan_2.21.8.tar.gz",   # Apparently this is available from local CRAN
#                    "/finngen/green/shinystan_2.5.0.tar.gz",
#                    #"/finngen/green/brms_2.16.3.tar.gz",    # Apparently this is available from local CRAN
#                    "/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/bayesian_1.0.1.tar.gz"),
#                  repos=NULL)
library(bayesian) #
library(tidymodels)
#install.packages("/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/broom.mixed_0.2.9.5.tar.gz", repos=NULL)
library(broom.mixed)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggridges)
library(tictoc)
library(performance)
library(survival)    # Installed by default
library(survminer)
# These packages are dependencies for detectseparation
# install.packages("pkgload")
# install.packages(c("/finngen/green/registry_0.5-1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/slam_0.1-50.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/ROI_1.0-0.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/lpSolveAPI_5.5.2.0-17.7.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/ROI.plugin.lpsolve_1.0-1.tar.gz")
#                  , repos=NULL)
library(detectseparation)
# These are dependencies for shapviz
# install.packages(c("/finngen/green/shades_1.4.0.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/ggfittext_0.10.2.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/gggenes_0.5.1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/xgboost_1.7.8.1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/shapviz_0.9.6.tar.gz"
#                    ),
#                  repos=NULL)

library(shapviz)
# install.packages("DiagrammeR")
# install.packages("/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/exclude_0.0.1.9000.tar.gz")
library(exclude) #
# These are dependencies for ciTools
# install.packages(c("/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/coda_0.19-4.1.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/arm_1.14-4.tar.gz",
#                    "/finngen/green/ciTools_0.6.1.tar.gz"), repos=NULL)
library(ciTools)
#install.packages("mboost")
#install.packages(c("/finngen/green/jarkko_upload/censored_0.3.2.tar.gz"), repos=NULL)
library(censored) #
#install.packages(c("statmod", "tableone", "forestplot"))
library(forestplot)
library(tableone)
library(gtsummary)
library(statmod)     # For quantile residuals
library(probably)    # For calibration plots

library(logistf)     # For Firth penelized logistic regression for small datas with imbalanced binary response

#install.packages("ranger)
source("common.R")

# This can measure time a chunk took to execute.
# Add chunk option time_it=TRUE to each chunk your want to measure.
knitr::knit_hooks$set(time_it = local({
  now <- NULL
  function(before, options) {
    if (before) {
      # record the current time before each chunk
      now <<- lubridate::now()
    } else {
      # calculate the time difference after a chunk
      res <- lubridate::now() - now
      # return a character string to show the time
      msg <- sprintf("Time for this code chunk to run: %.2f %s", as.numeric(res), units(res))
      message(msg)
      NULL   # Don't return the message so that it won't be printed to the resulting document.
      msg
    }
  }
}))
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=TRUE, time_it=TRUE)


#bgenix <- "~/progs/bgen.tgz/build/apps/bgenix"
bgenix <- "~/usr/bin/bgenix"

release <- "r13"; Release <- str_to_upper(release)

result_path <- file.path("../results", release)
cat(sprintf("result_path=%s", result_path), 
    sprintf("release=%s", release),
    sep = "\n",
    file = "../data/bash_init")  # Store the variables for bash chunks

#result_path <- "../results"
data_path <- "../data"
table_path  <- file.path(result_path, "tables")
figure_path <- file.path(result_path, "figures")
model_path  <- file.path(result_path, "models")
c(figure_path, table_path, model_path) %>% purrr::discard(dir.exists) %>% walk(~dir.create(., recursive = TRUE))  # Create dirs if they don't exist

boot.n <- 2000
recompute <- FALSE
recompute_bayes <- FALSE
save_figs <- TRUE
save_tables <- TRUE

credible_snip_names <- c("credible1", "credible2", "credible3")
known_snip_names <- c("H1_2", "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", "supersnip")
hla_snip_names <- c("rs114179634", "rs9261354", "rs3094093")
interacting_snip_names <- sprintf("int%i", 1:67)
# options(repos = "file:///usr/finngen-repos/cran/source")


```

Specify what performance measures we want to compute. Note the specification of the event_level.

```{r}
mymetrics <- metric_set(accuracy, kap, mn_log_loss, ppv,
                        yardstick::metric_tweak("roc_auc", roc_auc, event_level = "second"), 
                        yardstick::metric_tweak("pr_auc",   pr_auc, event_level = "second"),
                        yardstick::metric_tweak("brier_class", brier_class, event_level = "second")
                        )
# This is for xgboost as it has its response coded the other way ("case" is the first level of hemochromatosis)
mymetrics_rev <- metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, kap, mn_log_loss, ppv,
                        roc_auc, pr_auc)
```

## Functions

### Miscellaneous

```{r}
print_formula <- function(model) {
  model %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% formula() %>% deparse1() %>% cat("\n")
}
create_sex2 <- function(sex, age) {
  factor(case_when(sex == "male" ~ "male",
                   age <= 50     ~ "pre",
                   age > 50      ~ "post"),
         levels=c("male", "pre", "post"))
}
```


### Old stuff

```{r}
# These are old, don't use!

# It seems that the number of replicates must be at least as high as the number of rows in the dataframe
# https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-help/2011-February/269006.html
# boot.n is the number of bootstrap replications, if null use as many replications as there are rows in the dataframe
precision_recall_ci <- function(df, method="norm", boot.n=NULL) {
  
  get_aupr <- function(df, indices) {
    df2 <- df[indices,]
    pb$tick()  # update progress bar
    aupr <- PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0=df2$score, weights.class0=df2$original_label)$auc.davis.goadrich
    return(aupr)
  }
    
  df <- df %>% select(original_label, score)
  if (is.null(boot.n)) {
    boot.n <- nrow(df)
  }
  
  pb <- progress::progress_bar$new(total = boot.n+1)# init progress bar
  pb$tick(0)
  #p <- progress_estimated(n+1)  # init progress bar
  #b <- boot(df, statistic = get_aupr, R=boot.n, sim="ordinary", stype="i", strata=df$original_label, parallel="multicore")#, ncpus=1)
  b <- boot::boot(df, statistic = get_aupr, R=boot.n, sim="ordinary", stype="i", strata=df$original_label, parallel="no")#, ncpus=1)
  ret <- tryCatch(
    error = function(cnd) return(-1),
    {
      result <- boot::boot.ci(b, conf=0.95, type=method)
      var <- recode(method, "norm"="normal", "perc"="percent", "stud"="student")  # The name of the output field is stupidly sometimes not the same as the parameter name
      ci <- if (method=="norm") result[[var]][2:3] else result[[var]][4:5]
      NULL
    })
  if (!is.null(ret) && ret == -1) {
    ci <- C(NA, NA)
  }
  return(list(ci=ci, result=result))
}
  
create_precision_recall_new <- function(original_label, score, method="norm", boot.n=2000) {
  debug <- FALSE
  #message("Computing the precision-recall curve")
  pr_model     <- PRROC::pr.curve(scores.class0=score, weights.class0=original_label, curve=TRUE, rand.compute=TRUE)
  if (debug) message("hep1")
  points <- data.frame(pr_model$curve)
  if (debug) message("hep2")
  AUPR <- pr_model$auc.davis.goadrich
  df <- tibble(original_label=original_label, score=score)
  if (debug) message("hep3")
  prci <- precision_recall_ci(df, method=method, boot.n=boot.n)$ci
  if (debug) message("tassa1")
  title <- "Precision-recall"
  m <- mean(original_label)
  if (debug) message("tassa2")
  pr_plot <- ggplot(points, aes(x=X1,y=X2)) +
    geom_hline(aes(yintercept=m), color="lightgray") +   # theoretical PR curve of random classifier
    annotate(geom="text", label=sprintf("y=%.2f", m), x=0.25, y=m, vjust=-1) +
    geom_line() +
    annotate(geom="text", label=sprintf("AUPR: %.2f (%.2f%.2f)", AUPR, prci[1], prci[2]), x=0.5, y=0.875) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0.0, 1.0)) +
    labs(x="Recall",y="Precision", title=title)
  if (debug) message("tassa3")
  ci <- tibble("AUPR value"=AUPR, "AUPR low"=prci[1], "AUPR high"=prci[2])
  if (debug) message("tassa4")
  return(list(pr_plot=pr_plot, pr=points, pr_auc=AUPR, pr_ci=ci))
}

```

### Performance measures

```{r}
extract_predictions <- function(fit, new_data=NULL, parameters = NULL) {
  if (length(intersect(c("resample_results", "tune_results"), class(fit))) > 0) {
    pred <- fit %>%
      collect_predictions(parameters = parameters)
  } else {
    # Predict on the new data
    pred <- 
      fit %>% 
      augment(new_data)
  }
  pred
}
  
# Draws ROC and PR curves using either new_data or resamples
performance_curves <- function(fit, method, new_data=NULL, parameters = NULL, event_level = "second") {
  
  pred <- extract_predictions(fit, new_data, parameters)
  
  # Areas under the curves
  auroc <- pred %>% roc_auc(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% pull(.estimate) %>% sprintf("AUROC=%.2f", .)
  aupr  <- pred %>% pr_auc(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level)  %>% pull(.estimate) %>% sprintf("AUPR=%.2f", .)
  
  # ROC curve
  g1 <- pred %>%
    roc_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
    autoplot() +
    annotate(geom = "text", x=0.25, y=0, label=auroc) +
    labs(title=method, subtitle="ROC")
         
         # PR curve
  g2 <- pred %>%
    pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
    autoplot() +
    annotate(geom = "text", x=0.25, y=0, label=aupr) +
    labs(title=method, subtitle="PR")
  return(list(g1, g2))
}
```

### Risk table






Old version.

```{r}
risk_table <- function(lf, newdata, important_predictors, filename, title=NULL, n=16, conf.level = 0.95) {
  result <- compute_risk_cis(lf, newdata, important_predictors, count_data = train, conf.level = conf.level)
  result <- result %>%
    filter(n >= 5) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean))
  if (save_figs) {
    #png(filename, width=7, height=7, units="in", res=300)
    pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=basename(filename))
  }


  
  important_predictors2 <- c(important_predictors, 
                             Probability="probability", `Train count`="n")
  variables <- important_predictors2 %>% unname()
  header <- important_predictors2 %>% swap_names_and_values() # forestplot wants these in stupid order
  rlang::inject(  # forestplot function does not support injection operators !! and !!!
                  # Wrapping the whole pipeline in inject call makes them work.
  p <- result %>% 
    slice_max(order_by = mean, n=n) %>%
    mutate(probability = sprintf("%.3f", mean)) %>%
    forestplot(labeltext =  !!variables,  
               boxsize = 0.2,
               title = title,
               new_page = FALSE,
               xlab = "Hemochromatosis probability") %>%
    fp_add_lines() %>%
    fp_add_header(!!!header) %>% 
    # fp_add_header(sex2 = "Sex2",
    #               H63D = "H63D",
    #               C282Y = "C282Y",
    #               supersnip = "HLA3",
    #               probability = "Probability",
    #               n = "Train count")) %>%
    fp_set_zebra_style("#EFEFEF")
  )
  if (save_figs) {
    print(p)
    dev.off()
  }
  list(p=p, result=result)
}
```

Combined effect of C282Y and HLA3.

```{r}
# cis is a dataframe of effect sizes
C282Y_plus_HLA3 <- function(cis) {
  helper <- function(cis) { function(variable) { cis %>% filter(term == variable) %>% pull(estimate)} }
  f <- helper(cis)   # Function f gets the effect size of a variable from the cis dataframe
  tribble(
    ~C282Y, ~HLA3, ~`odds ratio`,
    0, 0, 1,
    0, 1, f("supersnip"),
    0, 2, f("supersnip")**2,
    1, 0, f("C282Y_X1"),
    1, 1, f("C282Y_X1") * f("supersnip")    * f("C282Y_X1_x_supersnip"),
    1, 2, f("C282Y_X1") * f("supersnip")**2 * f("C282Y_X1_x_supersnip")**2,
    2, 0, f("C282Y_X2"),
    2, 1, f("C282Y_X2") * f("supersnip")    * f("C282Y_X2_x_supersnip"),
    2, 2, f("C282Y_X2") * f("supersnip")**2 * f("C282Y_X2_x_supersnip")**2,
  )
}
```

### More old stuff


```{r, eval=FALSE}
# THIS DOES NOT WORK! I WANT TO SUM IN PROBABILITY SCALE, NOT IN LINK SCALE!
# Compute confidence intervals for the sum of responses in dataframe X2
confidence_interval_of_sum <- function(fit, X1, key, conf.level=0.95) {
  Vb <- vcov(fit)            # Variance-covariance matrix of regression coefficients
  X <- model.matrix(delete.response(terms(fit)), X1)
  Vy <- X %*% Vb %*% t(X)    # Variance-covariance matrix of predictions
  sum_of_estimates <- sum(predict(fit, newdata=X1, type="link"))
  # See https://stackoverflow.com/a/39338512 for how to compute standard error (and confidence interval)
  # for sum of predictions.
  variance_of_sum <- sum(Vy)
  alpha <- conf.level
  Qt <- c(-1, 1) * qt((1 - alpha) / 2, fit$df.residual, lower.tail = FALSE)
  #[1] -2.048407  2.048407
  
  ## %95 CI
  cis <- sum_of_estimates + Qt * sqrt(variance_of_sum)
  names(cis) <- c("lower", "upper")
  cis <- plogis(c(mean=sum_of_estimates, cis))   # Logistic function. Converts to probability
  bind_cols(
    as_tibble_row(cis),
    key
  )
}
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
# This computes confidence intervals for risks of sums of observations
sum_risk_table <- function(lf, newdata, counts, important_predictors, filename, title=NULL, n=16) {
  variables <- important_predictors %>% unname()
  ##fit <- lf %>% extract_fit_engine()
  
  
  confidence_helper <- function(lf, group, key) {
    baked <- lf %>% extract_recipe() %>% bake(group)
    confidence_interval_of_sum(lf %>% extract_fit_engine(), baked, key)
  }
  prediction_cis <- newdata %>%
    mutate(donation_bin = cut(donations_in_five_years, breaks = donation_breaks, right=FALSE)) %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(variables))) %>%
    group_map(function(group, key) confidence_helper(lf, group, key),
              .keep = TRUE) %>%
    bind_rows()
  
  # Compute confidence intervals for predictions
  # Note: We use confidence interval instead of prediction intervals, since for logistic regression
  # prediction intervals could only be [0,0], [1,1] or [0,1].
  # Same would apply for Poisson regression as well.
  # Confidence intervals don't have this problem as they are for the mean, not for a single observation.

  # prediction_cis <- lf %>%
  #   extract_recipe() %>%
  #   bake(newdata) %>% 
  #   ciTools::add_ci(fit, names = c("lower", "upper"), yhatName = "mean", alpha = 0.05) %>%
  #   mutate(type = "parametric") %>% 
  #   as_tibble()
  # counts <- train %>% count(across(all_of(variables)))
  result <- 
    # bind_cols(
    #   newdata %>% select(all_of(variables)),
    #   prediction_cis %>% select(mean, lower, upper)) %>% 
    prediction_cis %>%
    left_join(counts, by=variables) %>%
    replace_na(list(n=0)) %>%
    filter(n >= 5) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean))
  if (save_figs) {
    pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=basename(filename))
  }

  important_predictors2 <- c(important_predictors, 
                             Probability="probability", `Train count`="n")
  variables2 <- important_predictors2 %>% unname()
  header <- important_predictors2 %>% swap_names_and_values() # forestplot wants these in stupid order
  rlang::inject(  # forestplot function does not support injection operators !! and !!!
                  # Wrapping the whole pipeline in inject call makes them work.
    p <- result %>% 
      slice_max(order_by = mean, n=n) %>%
      mutate(probability = sprintf("%.6f", mean)) %>%
      forestplot(labeltext =  !!variables2,  
                 boxsize = 0.2,
                 title = title,
                 new_page = FALSE,
                 xlab = "Hemochromatosis probability") %>%
      fp_add_lines() %>%
      fp_add_header(!!!header) %>% 
      fp_set_zebra_style("#EFEFEF")
  )
  #g
  if (save_figs) {
    print(p)
    dev.off()
  }
  list(p=p, result=result)
}
```


## Load data 

### All data

```{r}
L <- readRDS(file.path(result_path, "all_datas.rds"))
```



### Prior diseases

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "prior_disease_events.tsv")
prior_disease_events <- read_tsv(filename)
disease_names <- setdiff(colnames(prior_disease_events), "sample")
```

These are either 1 or 5 years before the first HC diagnosis, depending on the disease. See the top10 table in the
finngen_explore.html.

### FinnGen hemochromatosis credible snips

```{r}
filename <- file.path(data_path, "credible_snip_info.tsv")
credible_snip_info <- read_tsv(filename)
```

Read dosages.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "tidy_credible.dosages.tsv.gz")
credible_snips <- read_tsv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE)
credible_snip_names <- c("credible1", "credible2", "credible3")
credible_snips %>% select(-sample) %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>% summary()
```

### Significant snips from HLA area


```{r}
filename <- file.path(data_path, "hla_snip_info.tsv")
hla_snip_info <- read_tsv(filename)
```




Read dosages.

```{r Read dosages2}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "tidy_hla_dosages.tsv.gz")
if (! file.exists(filename)) {
  tmp <- read_delim(output, delim=" ", col_types = cols(position = col_integer(), .default = col_character()))
  tmp <- tmp %>% 
    select(-c(chromosome, rsid, position, alleleA, alleleB)) %>% 
    inner_join(hla_snip_info %>% select(SNPID, rsid), by="SNPID") %>%
    select(-SNPID) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols=-c("rsid"), names_to = "sample", values_to = "dosage") %>% 
    mutate(dosage = as.integer(dosage)) #%>%
  #inner_join(credible_snips %>% select(SNPID="locus_id", name), by="SNPID")
  #dosages_wide <- dosages %>% pivot_wider(names_from=name, values_from=dosage, id_cols = sample)
  hla_snips <- tmp %>% pivot_wider(names_from=rsid, values_from=dosage, id_cols = sample)
  write_tsv(hla_snips, filename)
} else  {
  hla_snips <- read_tsv(filename)
}
hla_snip_names <- hla_snip_info$rsid
hla_snips %>% select(-sample) %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>% summary()
```





### Well-known snips from literature

```{r}
known_snip_info <- tibble::tribble(
               ~SNPID,       ~name,       ~rsid, ~chromosome, ~position, ~first, ~second,
  "chr6_26071867_A_C",      "H1-2", "rs9968910",           6,  26071867,    "A",     "C",
  "chr6_26090951_C_G",      "H63D", "rs1799945",           6,  26090951,    "C",     "G",
  "chr6_26090957_A_T",      "S65C", "rs1800730",           6,  26090957,    "A",     "T",
  "chr6_26092913_G_A",     "C282Y", "rs1800562",           6,  26092913,    "G",     "A",
  "chr6_32636375_A_G", "supersnip", "rs9272324",           6,  32636375,    "A",     "G"
  )
#known_snip_names <- known_snip_info$name
```

Get dosages.

```{r}
known_snips <- L$dosages %>% pivot_wider(names_from=name, values_from=dosage, id_cols=sample) %>%
  rename(H1_2="H1-2")
known_snips %>% select(-sample) %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>% summary()
```

### Extra snips

```{r}
extra_snips_info <- tribble(
  ~SNPID, ~name, ~rsid, ~chromosome, ~pos_hg38, ~first, ~second,
  "chr6_24111389_T_C", "extra1", "rs1419230", 6, 24111389, "T", "C"
)
extra_snips_filename <- "../data/extra.tsv"
if (! file.exists(extra_snips_filename))
  write_tsv(extra_snips_info %>% select(SNPID), extra_snips_filename, col_names = FALSE)
```

Extract the variants.

```{r Extract variants3}
if (release == "r12") {
  input <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/bgen_2.0/data/chrom/finngen_R12_6.bgen"
} else {
  input <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/bgen_1.0/data/chrom/finngen_R13_6.bgen"
}
#bgen_base <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/bgen_2.0/data/chrom"
#input_bgen <- file.path(bgen_base, "finngen_R12_6.bgen")
input_sample <- paste0(input, ".sample")
extra_bgen <- file.path(result_path, "extra.bgen")
extra_dosage <- file.path(result_path, "extra.dosage")

# Use bgenix, it is faster
cmd <- glue("{bgenix} -g {input} -incl-rsids {extra_snips_filename} > {extra_bgen}")

print(cmd)
if (!file.exists(extra_bgen)) {
  system(cmd)
}
# Create the index
cmd <- glue("{bgenix} -g {extra_bgen} -index")
print(cmd)
if (!file.exists(paste0(extra_bgen, ".bgi"))) {
  system(cmd)
}
```

qctool took 9m16.454s
bgenix took 0m4.236s

Convert bgen to dosage file.

```{r Extract dosages3}
cmd <- glue("qctool -g {extra_bgen} -og {extra_dosage} -ofiletype dosage -incl-snpids {extra_snips_filename}")
print(cmd)
if (!file.exists(extra_dosage)) {
  system(cmd)
}
```

Read dosages.

```{r Read snip dosages}
extra_snips <- read_delim(extra_dosage, delim=" ", col_types = cols(position = col_integer(), .default = col_character()))
extra_snips <- extra_snips %>% 
  select(-c(chromosome, rsid, position, alleleA, alleleB)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols=-c("SNPID"), names_to = "sample", values_to = "dosage") %>% 
  mutate(dosage = as.integer(dosage)) %>%
  inner_join(extra_snips_info %>% select(SNPID, rsid, name), by="SNPID")
extra_snips_wide <- extra_snips %>% pivot_wider(names_from=name, values_from=dosage, id_cols = sample)
```

### All snips

Combine all snip information in a table
```{r}
all_snip_info <- bind_rows(
  known_snip_info %>% rename(ref = first, alt = second, pos_hg38 = position),
  credible_snip_info %>% rename(ref = first, alt = second),
  hla_snip_info %>% rename(chromosome = chrom)
) %>% select(SNPID, name, rsid, chromosome, pos_hg38, ref, alt)
if (save_tables)
  write_tsv(all_snip_info, file.path(table_path, "all_snip_info.tsv"))
all_snip_info
```

### Interacting snips

Extract the variants with bgenix.

```{r Extract variants 4}
#bgen_base <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/bgen_2.0/data/chrom"
input_bgen <- if (release == "r12") "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/bgen_2.0/data/chrom/finngen_R12_6.bgen" else "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/bgen_1.0/data/chrom/finngen_R13_6.bgen"
input_sample <- paste0(input_bgen, ".sample")
output <- file.path(result_path, "interacting_snips.dosage")

snip_id_filename <- file.path(result_path, "interacting_snips_ids.tsv")
cmd <- glue("{bgenix} -g {input_bgen} -incl-rsids {snip_id_filename} | ",
            "qctool -g - -filetype bgen -og {output} -ofiletype dosage")
print(cmd)
if (!file.exists(output)) {
  system(cmd)
}
```



```{r Read interaction dosages}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "tidy_interacting.dosages.tsv.gz")
if (! file.exists(filename)) {
  input <- file.path(result_path, "interacting_snips.dosage")
  tmp <- read_delim(input, delim=" ", col_types = cols(position = col_integer(), .default = col_character()))
  # Cleanup
  interacting_snip_names <- sprintf("int%i", 1:nrow(tmp))   # 67 snips
  tmp <- tmp %>% 
    mutate(name = interacting_snip_names) %>%
    select(-c(chromosome, rsid, position, alleleA, alleleB)) %>% 
    #inner_join(credible_snips_info %>% select(SNPID, name), by="SNPID") %>%
    pivot_longer(cols=-c("SNPID", "name"), names_to = "sample", values_to = "dosage") %>% 
    mutate(dosage = as.numeric(dosage))
    # mutate(dosage = as.integer(round(dosage))) #%>%  # no need for round as XGboost is used
  #inner_join(credible_snips %>% select(SNPID="locus_id", name), by="SNPID")
  #tmp_wide <- tmp %>% pivot_wider(names_from=name, values_from=dosage, id_cols = sample)
  interacting_snips <- tmp %>% pivot_wider(names_from=name, values_from=dosage, id_cols = sample)
  write_tsv(interacting_snips, filename)
} else {
  interacting_snips <- read_tsv(filename)
}
interacting_snips %>% select(-sample) %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>% summary()
```


### Blood donation data

```{r}
#filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/analysis_covariates/R12_COV_V2.FID.txt.gz"
if (release == "r12") {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/phenotype_1.0/data/finngen_R12_minimum_1.0.txt.gz"
} else {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/phenotype_1.0/data/finngen_R13_minimum_1.0.txt.gz"
}
dob <- read_tsv(filename, col_select = c(sample=FINNGENID, APPROX_BIRTH_DATE))
L$hc <- L$hc %>% left_join(dob, by="sample")
L$hc <- L$hc %>% mutate(approx_event_death_eof_date = as_date(APPROX_BIRTH_DATE + dyears(AGE)))
# Covariate information is not available for outliers.
```

```{r}
if (release == "r12") {
  #filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/analysis_covariates/R12_COV_V2.FID.txt.gz"
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R11/analysis_covariates/R11_COV_V0.FID.txt.gz"
} else {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/analysis_covariates/data/R13_COV_V0.FID.txt.gz"
}
sex_blood <- read_tsv(filename, col_select = c("FID", "SEX", "SEX_IMPUTED")) %>% rename(sample=FID) %>% mutate(across(SEX, as.factor))
```

```{r}
if (release == "r12") {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R11/phenotype_1.0/data/finngen_R11_minimum_extended_1.0.txt.gz"
} else {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/phenotype_1.0/data/finngen_R13_minimum_extended_1.0.txt.gz"
}
sex_blood_all <- read_tsv(filename, col_select = c("FINNGENID", "SEX")) %>% rename(sample=FINNGENID) %>% mutate(across(SEX, as.factor))
```

Read blood donation data.

```{r}
filename <- if (release == "r12") "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R11/FRCBS_blood_donor_data_1.0/data/finngen_R11_FRCBS_Blood_Donor_Donations_1.0.txt.gz" else "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/FRCBS_blood_donor_data/data/finngen_R13_FRCBS_Blood_Donor_Donations_1.0.txt.gz"
#filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R11/FRCBS_blood_donor_data_1.0/data/finngen_R11_FRCBS_Blood_Donor_Donations_1.0.txt.gz"
blood_donations <- read_tsv(filename)
blood_donations <- blood_donations %>% rename(sample = FINNGENID) %>% mutate(across(BloodDonationTypeKey, as.factor))
```

Sanity check:

```{r}
tmp <- blood_donations %>% filter(BloodDonationTypeKey=="Whole_blood_donation") %>% left_join(sex_blood_all, by="sample") %>%
  mutate(low = (SEX=="female" & Hb < 125) | (SEX=="male" & Hb < 135))
summary(tmp)
```

Frequencies when NAs are removed.

```{r}
tmp %>% group_by(SEX) %>% 
  summarise(low=mean(low, na.rm=TRUE))
```

Frequencies when NAs in Hb are considered Hb deferral.

```{r}
tmp %>% group_by(SEX) %>% 
  mutate(low = coalesce(low, TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(low=mean(low))
```

Last donation is from 2021-12-14.

```{r}
sprintf("Last donation is from %s\n", max(blood_donations$APPROX_DONATION_DATE))
```

Whereas the followup ends on 2023-05-15.

```{r}
sprintf("Followup ends on %s\n", max(L$hc$approx_event_death_eof_date, na.rm=TRUE))
```



```{r}
plot_hb_distributions <- function(blood_donations, sex, title) {
  donations <- blood_donations %>% filter(BloodDonationTypeKey == "Whole_blood_donation")
  donations2 <- donations %>% left_join(sex, by="sample")
  rejected <- donations2 %>% filter(! is.na(Hb)) %>%
    mutate(rejected=(SEX=="female" & Hb < 125) | (SEX=="male" & Hb < 135) | is.na(Hb))
  rejected_counts <- rejected %>% group_by(SEX) %>% summarise(n = sum(rejected), fraction=mean(rejected))
  donations2 %>% 
    ggplot(aes(Hb, fill=SEX)) + geom_histogram() + 
    geom_vline(mapping=aes(xintercept=Hb), data=tibble(Hb = c(125, 135), SEX=c("female", "male"))) +
    geom_text(mapping=aes(x=I(0.0), y=I(0.9), label=sprintf("rejected %i (%.1f%%)", n, fraction*100)), data=rejected_counts, hjust=0) +
    labs(y="Count", title=title) +
    facet_grid(~ SEX)
}
```

```{r}
plot_hb_distributions(blood_donations, sex_blood, "Blood donations sex")
```

```{r}
plot_hb_distributions(blood_donations, sex_blood_all, "Blood donations sex_R all")
```

Get number of donations before the first event, death or end of followup.
Five year window.

```{r}
x_year_sliding_window_sum <- function(weight, date, x=2) {
  v <- as.numeric(slider::slide_index(weight, date, sum, .before = lubridate::dyears(x))) # years(2) had problems with leap days
  return(v)
}
```


```{r}
helper <- function(df, key) {
  if (nrow(df) > 1) {
    df <- df %>% drop_na(Hb)
  }
  if (nrow(df) == 1) {
    
  }
  stopifnot(nrow(df) == 1)
  df
}
statistics <- function(df) { list(donations = nrow(df), donors=n_distinct(df$sample)) }
filename <- file.path(result_path, "processed_donations.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  donations <- blood_donations %>% 
    #head(10000) %>%
    init_exclude(statistics = statistics) %>%
    inner_join(sex_blood_all, by="sample") %>%
    exclude("Inner join sex") %>%
    filter(BloodDonationTypeKey == "Whole_blood_donation") %>%
    exclude("Exclude non-whole-blood-donations") %>%
    distinct() %>%
    exclude("Remove duplicate rows") %>%
    group_by(sample, APPROX_DONATION_DATE) %>%
    group_modify(helper) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    exclude("Selected one donation per day from a donor") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Hb)) %>%
    exclude("Exclude NA Hb values") %>%
    filter((SEX=="female" & Hb >= 125) | (SEX=="male" & Hb >= 135)) %>%
    exclude("Exclude hb-deferrals") %>%
    left_join(dob, by="sample") %>%
    exclude("Left join date of birth") %>%
    mutate(donation_age = interval(APPROX_BIRTH_DATE, APPROX_DONATION_DATE)/years(1)) %>%
    select(-c(BloodDonationTypeKey, APPROX_BIRTH_DATE)) %>%
    arrange(APPROX_DONATION_DATE) %>%
    mutate(dummy = 1, across(APPROX_DONATION_DATE, as_datetime))  %>% # For computing the donations in five years
    group_by(sample) %>%
    mutate(donations_in_five_years = x_year_sliding_window_sum(dummy, APPROX_DONATION_DATE, x=5)) %>%  # The number includes the current donation
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-dummy) %>%
    mutate(across(APPROX_DONATION_DATE, as_date))
  saveRDS(donations, filename)
  exclusions_filename <- file.path(table_path, "donation_exclusions.tsv")
  e <- get_exclude()
  donation_exclusions <- as_tibble(e)
  write_tsv(donation_exclusions, exclusions_filename)
} else {
  donations <- readRDS(filename)
}



donation_count <- donations %>%
  inner_join(L$hc %>% select(sample, approx_event_death_eof_date), by="sample") %>%
  filter(APPROX_DONATION_DATE < approx_event_death_eof_date,
         APPROX_DONATION_DATE >= approx_event_death_eof_date - dyears(5)) %>%
  count(sample, name="donations_in_five_years")
```



```{r}
n_distinct(blood_donations$sample)
n_distinct(donations$sample)
n_distinct(donation_count$sample)
```

It seems that large number of donors drop because they have no donations in the two-year window.

### Read end of follow-up age

Read death / end of follow-up age.

```{r}
if (release == "r12") {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R12/analysis_covariates/R12_COV_V2.FID.txt.gz"
} else {
  filename <- "/finngen/library-red/finngen_R13/analysis_covariates/data/R13_COV_V0.FID.txt.gz"
}
EOF_AGE <- read_tsv(filename, col_select = c("FID", "AGE_AT_DEATH_OR_END_OF_FOLLOWUP")) %>%
  rename(sample=FID)
```


### Form data

Join data sets.

```{r}
basic <- L$hc %>%
  select(sample, hemochromatosis, event_age=AGE, approx_event_date = APPROX_EVENT_DAY, approx_event_death_eof_date) %>%
  inner_join(L$bd, by="sample") %>%
  inner_join(L$sex %>% rename(sex=SEX), by="sample") %>%
  left_join(EOF_AGE, by="sample") %>%  
  mutate(age = AGE_AT_DEATH_OR_END_OF_FOLLOWUP) %>%
  mutate(sex2 = create_sex2(sex, age),
         sex = factor(sex, levels=c("male", "female"))) %>%
  left_join(prior_disease_events, by="sample") %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(disease_names), ~ replace_na(., FALSE))) %>%
  left_join(credible_snips, by="sample") %>%
  left_join(known_snips, by="sample") %>%
  left_join(hla_snips, by="sample") %>%
  left_join(interacting_snips, by="sample") %>%
  left_join(donation_count, by="sample") %>%
  replace_na(list(donations_in_five_years=0)) %>%
  mutate(hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))
```


```{r}
basic %>% 
  select(-sample) %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(c(credible_snip_names, known_snip_names, hla_snip_names)), as.factor)) %>% 
  summary()
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "basic.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  saveRDS(basic, filename)
} else {
  basic <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
basic <- basic %>% 
  #select(-(int1:int67)) %>%
  left_join(EOF_AGE, by="sample") %>%
  mutate(age = AGE_AT_DEATH_OR_END_OF_FOLLOWUP,
         sex2 = create_sex2(sex, age))   # We need to recreate this
```


## Split

Divide the data into train and test sets.

```{r}
seed <- 142
set.seed(seed)
helper <- function(n) {
  v <- rep(c("train", "test"), length.out=n)
  sample(v, n)
}
use_tidymodels <- TRUE
if (use_tidymodels) {
  # See
  # https://github.com/tidymodels/rsample/issues/473
  basic_split <- basic %>%
    initial_split(strata = "hemochromatosis", prop = 0.5)   #, pool = 0.0010)  # This causes bias
  #set.seed(seed)
  #basic_split_wild <- basic %>%
  #  filter(C282Y == 0) %>%
  #  initial_split(strata = "hemochromatosis", prop = 0.5, pool = 0.0010)
  #initial_validation_split(strata = "hemochromatosis", prop = c(0.6, 0.2))
  train <- training(basic_split)
  test  <- testing(basic_split)
  #train_wild <- training(basic_split_wild)
  #test_wild  <- testing(basic_split_wild)
} else {
  tmp <- basic %>% 
    group_by(hemochromatosis, sex) %>%
    mutate(group = helper(n())) %>%
    ungroup() #%>%
  #mutate(hemochromatosis = factor(if_else(hemochromatosis == "case", 1, 0)))
  train <- tmp %>% filter(group=="train") %>% select(-group)
  test  <- tmp %>% filter(group=="test") %>% select(-group)
  rm(tmp)
}
```

Save training sample ids for HLA hemochromatosis GWAS.

```{r, eval=recompute}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "training_sample_ids.tsv.gz")
if (save_tables)
  write_tsv(train %>% select(FID=sample), filename)
```


```{r}
sprintf("Train: %i cases, %i controls, %.2f%% prevalence", sum(train$hemochromatosis=="case"), sum(train$hemochromatosis=="control"), mean(train$hemochromatosis=="case")*100)
sprintf("Test: %i cases, %i controls, %.2f%% prevalence", sum(test$hemochromatosis=="case"), sum(test$hemochromatosis=="control"), mean(test$hemochromatosis=="case")*100)
```

Opposite response coding for xgboost to get correct effect directions of shap values.

```{r}
genetic_variables <- c("H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", "supersnip", "credible1", "credible2", "credible3", "rs3094093", "rs114179634")
xg_train <- train %>% mutate(across(all_of(genetic_variables), as.factor),
                             hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("case", "control")))
xg_test <- test %>% mutate(across(all_of(genetic_variables), as.factor),
                             hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("case", "control")))
```

Form folds for cross-validation.

```{r}
number_of_folds <- 10
set.seed(345)
folds <- vfold_cv(train, v = number_of_folds)
set.seed(345)
xg_folds <- vfold_cv(xg_train, v = number_of_folds)
```


## Checking additivity

```{r}
check_additivity <- function(data, snip, method="glm.fit", covariates=c()) {
  data2 <- data %>% mutate(additive=.data[[snip]], 
                           deviation=as.integer(.data[[snip]]==2))
                           #hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))
  s <- glue("hemochromatosis ~ additive + deviation")
  if (length(covariates) > 0)
    s <- paste(c(s, covariates), collapse = " + ")
  f <- as.formula(s)
  m <- glm(f, data=data2, family = "binomial", method=method)
  m
}
#m <- check_additivity(train, "C282Y")
```

### Without covariates

```{r}
tmp <- known_snip_names %>% set_names() %>% 
  map(function(snip) {
    check_additivity(train, snip) %>% tidy(conf.int=TRUE, exponentiate=TRUE)
  })
additivity_effects <- tmp %>% bind_rows(.id = "snip") %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")
```

Why S65C fails?

```{r}
known_snip_names %>% map(function(snip) train %>% select(hemochromatosis, all_of(snip)) %>% table())
```

Probably S65C fails because variable S65C==2 decides hemochromatosis status nearly completely, 
a quasi-complete separation issue.

```{r Detect for separation, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "detect_separation.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename)) {
  tic("Detect separation")
  result_ds <- known_snip_names %>% set_names() %>% 
    map(function(snip) {
      check_additivity(train, snip, method="detect_separation")
    })
  toc()
  saveRDS(result_ds, filename)
} else {
  result_ds <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

detectseparation package also detects separation for the deviation parameter for S65C.


```{r}
plot_additivity_effects <- function(addivity_effects) {
  additivity_effects %>% 
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels=c("deviation", "additive")),
           color = if_else(p.value < 0.05, "red", "black")) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=estimate, xmin=conf.low, xmax=conf.high, y=term)) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray") +
    geom_pointrange() + 
    geom_text(aes(label=sprintf("p=%.2e", p.value), color=I(color)), hjust=0, nudge_y = 0.25) +
    facet_wrap(~snip, scales = "free_x")
}
```

```{r}
plot_additivity_effects(addivity_effects)
```

So, C282Y and H1-2 snips seem to deviate from additive behaviour.


```{r}
which_model <- function(df, snip2) {
  effect <- df %>% filter(snip==snip2) %>% select(term, estimate) %>% deframe()
  heterozygote <- effect[["additive"]]
  homozygote <- effect[["additive"]]**2 * effect[["deviation"]]
  cat(sprintf("Being heterozygote in %s multiplies odds by %f\n", snip2, heterozygote))
  cat(sprintf("Being homozygote in %s multiplies odds by %f\n", snip2, homozygote))
  if (heterozygote < homozygote) {
    cat(sprintf("Therefore, recessive model should be used for %s\n", snip2))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("Therefore, dominant model should be used for %s\n", snip2))
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}
```


```{r}
which_model(additivity_effects, "C282Y")
which_model(additivity_effects, "H1_2")
```

### With covariates

```{r}
tmp <- known_snip_names %>% set_names() %>% 
  map(function(snip) {
    check_additivity(train, snip, covariates=c("sex", "approx_event_death_eof_date")) %>% 
      tidy(conf.int=TRUE, exponentiate=TRUE)
  })
additivity_effects_with_covariates <- tmp %>% bind_rows(.id = "snip") %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)")
```

```{r}
plot_additivity_effects(addivity_effects_with_covariates)
```

```{r}
which_model(additivity_effects_with_covariates, "C282Y")
which_model(additivity_effects_with_covariates, "H1_2")
```

Adding covariates did not have a large effect.

## Model 0

This is the baseline model.

### Basic logistic model


```{r}
variables0 <- c("sex2", "age",
                "H63D", "C282Y"
                )
f0 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables0, collapse=" + "))
f0
```

```{r logistic-mod-0}
logistic_mod_0 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_0
```


```{r logistic_recipe-0}
logistic_recipe_0 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f0), data = train) %>% 
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y and H63D as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D")   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
logistic_recipe_0
```


```{r logistic-workflow-0}
logistic_wflow_0 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_0) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_0)
logistic_wflow_0
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-0}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_0.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_0 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_0, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_0, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_0 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_0)
```

```{r full-fit-0, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_full_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_full_5 <- fit(logistic_wflow_5, basic)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```




Show trained recipe.

```{r}
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```

The step_lincomb removed two interactions!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


Basic info about the fit.

```{r glance0}
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```

```{r}
tic()
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% plot()
toc()
```

This one uses jackknife deviance residuals.

```{r}
tic()
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% boot::glm.diag.plots()
toc()
```

Try with quantile residuals instead.

```{r}
constant_information_transformation <- function(fit) {
  family <- fit$family$family
  # Constant-information scale transformation of the linear predictor.
  # See page 308 of Generalized linear models with examples in R
  transformation <- if (family == "binomial") {
    function(x) asin(sqrt(x))
  } else if (family == "poisson") {
    function(x) sqrt(x)
  } else if (family == "gamma") {
    function(x) log(x)
  } else {
    function(x) { stop("not implemented") }
  }
  transformation  
}
# This analyses the random component of a GLM model
my_diagnostics <- function(fit) {
  transformation <- constant_information_transformation(fit)
  par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
  qr <- qresid(fit)
  qqnorm(qr, las=1); qqline(qr)
  plot(qr ~ transformation(fitted(fit)), las=1)   # sin^-1(sqrt(mu)) is the constant-information scale transformation for Binomial distribution    
  plot(cooks.distance(fit), type="h", las=1)
  plot(hatvalues(fit), type="h", las=1)
}
fit <- logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_fit_engine()
my_diagnostics(fit)
```

```{r}
# These analyse the systematic component of a GLM
my_diagnostics2 <- function(fit) {
  transformation <- constant_information_transformation(fit)
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  scatter.smooth(rstandard(fit) ~ transformation(fitted(fit)), las=1, 
                 ylab="Standardized deviance residuals", xlab="log(Fitted values)")
  z <- resid(fit, type="working") + fit$linear.predictors
  plot(z ~ fit$linear.predictors, las=1, xlab="Working responses", ylab="Linear predictor")
  abline(0, 1)
}
my_diagnostics2(fit)
```


```{r}
par(mfrow = c(3, 3))
termplot(fit, data=fit$data, partial.resid = TRUE, las=1)
```

```{r}
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-0}
logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

Calibration.

```{r}
logistic_lf_0_test_pred <- augment(logistic_lf_0)
logistic_lf_0_test_pred %>%
  cal_plot_windowed(truth = hemochromatosis, estimate = .pred_case, step_size = 0.025, event_level="second")
```




```{r logistic-cis-0}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_0.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_0 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_0, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_0 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-plot-0}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_0.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_0, "Logistic model 0 FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_0)) + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-0, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_full_0.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_full_0 <- compute_cis(logistic_full_0)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_full_0, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_full_0 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-plot-0, eval=FALSE}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_0.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_full_0, "Logistic model 0 full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_full_0)) + labs(y="Variable")
#ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```


### Cox regression

```{r}
baseline_variables0 <- c("sex2", 
                        "H63D",
                        "C282Y", 
                        "C282Y:H63D")
variables0_cox <- c(baseline_variables0)
f0_cox <- as.formula(sprintf("Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ %s", paste(variables0_cox, collapse=" + ")))
f0_cox
```

```{r}
cox_train <- train %>% mutate(hemochromatosis = as.integer(hemochromatosis == "case"),
                              across(c(C282Y, H63D), as.factor))
cox_full <- basic %>% mutate(hemochromatosis = as.integer(hemochromatosis == "case"),
                              across(c(C282Y, H63D), as.factor))
```


```{r}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_fit_0.rds", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_fit_0 <- survival::coxph(f0_cox, data=cox_train)
  saveRDS(cox_fit_0, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_fit_0 <- readRDS(filename)
}
summary(cox_fit_0)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_full_0", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_full_0 <- survival::coxph(f0_cox, data=cox_full)
  saveRDS(cox_full_0, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_full_0 <- readRDS(filename)
}
summary(cox_full_0)
```

Nothing interesting in this.

```{r}
g <- survminer::ggsurvplot(survfit(cox_fit_0), cox_train, risk.table = TRUE)
grid.newpage()
print(g, newpage=FALSE)
```




```{r Compute basic Cox model effect sizes 0}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_0.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_cis_0 <- tidy(cox_fit_0, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_cis_0, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_cis_0 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot basic Cox model effect sizes 0}
g <- plot_cis(cox_cis_0, "Cox model 0 FinnGen") +
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_0.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

```{r Compute basic full Cox model effect sizes 0, eval=FALSE}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_full_0.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_cis_full_0 <- tidy(cox_full_0, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_cis_full_0, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_cis_full_0 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot basic full Cox model effect sizes 0, eval=FALSE}
g <- plot_cis(cox_cis_full_0, "Cox model 0 full FinnGen") +
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_full_0.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

## Model 1

This model is supposed to be comparable to the UKBB model. Cannot split women in to pre and postmenopausal since
in UKBB age is always at least 40.

```{r}
variables1 <- c("sex", "age", "H1_2", "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                #"H63D:C282Y", # This needs to be done in recipe
                "supersnip", "rs3094093", "rs114179634")
f1 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables1, collapse=" + "))
f1
```



### Logistic regression model

```{r}
logistic_mod_1 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_1
```


```{r}
logistic_recipe_1 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f1), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_1
```


```{r}
#logistic_fit_1 <- logistic_mod_1 %>%
#  fit(as.formula(f1), data = model_train_data)
logistic_wflow_1 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_1) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_1)
logistic_wflow_1
```

Actual model fitting.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_ff_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_fit_1 <- logistic_wflow_1 %>%
    fit(data=train)
  saveRDS(logistic_fit_1, filename)
} else {
  logistic_fit_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
logistic_fit_1
```




```{r}
#| layout-ncol: 2
res <- performance_curves(logistic_fit_1, "Logistic regression 1 train", train)
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_1 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_1, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_1, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```


```{r}
class(logistic_lf_1)
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_1)
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_1 <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_1, times=10, metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 1")
logistic_perf_1
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
lm_est <- function(split, ...) {
  lm(f1, data = analysis(split)) %>%
    tidy()
}

lm_est2 <- function(split, ...) {
  logistic_wflow_1 %>%
    fit(data=analysis(split)) %>%
    tidy()
}

set.seed(52156)
conf_boot <-
  bootstraps(train, 10, apparent = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(results = map(splits, lm_est))

```

Following does not work.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_fit_1)
```

Basic info about the fit.

```{r}
broom::glance(logistic_fit_1)
```



```{r}
logistic_lf_1 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```


Variance inflation factors. No inflation.

```{r}
logistic_fit_1 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

The below crashes the notebook!!!

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_fit_1 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% plot()
```

R2 value:

```{r}
logistic_lf_1 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_1.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_1 <- compute_cis(logistic_fit_1)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_1, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_1 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```



```{r}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_1.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_1, "Logistic model 1", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_1))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
g
```

### Random forest model

```{r}
rf_mod_1 <- parsnip::rand_forest() %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance="impurity") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
rf_mod_1
```


```{r}
rf_recipe_1 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f1), data = train) %>% 
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) #%>%
  #step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y)     # No interaction for random forest models
rf_recipe_1
```


```{r}
rf_wflow_1 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(rf_mod_1) %>% 
  add_recipe(rf_recipe_1)
rf_wflow_1
```


```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "rf_ff_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  rf_fit_1 <- rf_wflow_1 %>%
    fit(data=train)
  saveRDS(rf_fit_1, filename)
} else {
  rf_fit_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
# Don't print the ranger object directly as its printout is large and will crash Rstudio!!!!!!!!!!!!
rf_fit_1 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% summary()
```

```{r}
res <- performance_curves(rf_fit_1, "Random forest 1 train", train)
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```



This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "rf_lf_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  rf_lf_1 <- last_fit(rf_wflow_1, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(rf_lf_1, filename)
} else {
  rf_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(rf_lf_1)
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(rf_lf_1)
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
rf_perf_1 <- int_pctl(rf_lf_1, times=10, metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>%
  mutate(model="Random forest 1")
rf_perf_1
```

Basic info about the fit. Not available for ranger object.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
broom::glance(rf_fit_1)
```

```{r}
rf_lf_1 %>%
  extract_workflow() %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  vip(geom="point") +
  labs(title="Random forest 1")
```

### Lasso logistic regression

```{r}
# Create validation set
val_set <- validation_split(train, 
                            strata = hemochromatosis, 
                            prop = 0.50)
# Specify the model
lasso_mod_1 <- 
  logistic_reg(penalty = tune(), mixture = 1) %>%  # Mixture of 1 causes Lasso to be used
  set_engine("glmnet")
# Workflow
lasso_wflow_1 <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_1) %>%
  add_model(lasso_mod_1)
# Penalty grid
lasso_grid <- tibble(penalty = 10^seq(-4, -1, length.out = 30))
# Tune the penalty parameter
lasso_tune_1 <- 
  #lasso_mod_1 %>% 
  lasso_wflow_1 %>% 
  tune_grid(#preprocessor=as.formula(f1),
            val_set,
            grid = lasso_grid,
            control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, save_workflow = TRUE),
            metrics = metric_set(roc_auc))
```

```{r}
lasso_tune_1 %>% collect_metrics()
```

```{r}
lasso_tune_1 %>% compute_metrics(metric_set(pr_auc, roc_auc))
```

```{r}
best_parameters <- lasso_tune_1 %>% select_best(metric = "roc_auc")
best_penalty <- best_parameters %>% pull(penalty)
```


Lasso does not seem to help.

```{r}
res <- int_pctl(lasso_tune_1, 
                metrics = metric_set(roc_auc, pr_auc), 
                times=100,                   # For speed 
                parameters=lasso_grid %>% head(n=5) # For speed
                )
res
```



```{r}
#lasso_tune_1 %>% 
res %>%
  filter(.metric == "roc_auc") %>%
  #collect_metrics() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = penalty, y = .estimate)) + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = .lower, ymax = .upper), fill="red") +
  geom_point() + 
  geom_line() + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = best_penalty) +
  ylab("Area under the ROC Curve") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_number())
```


```{r}
res <- performance_curves(lasso_tune_1, "Lasso regression 1 train", new_data=train, parameters=best_parameters)
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "lasso_ff_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  lasso_fit_1 <- lasso_tune_1 %>%
    fit_best(parameters = best_parameters, verbose = TRUE, add_validation_set = TRUE)
  saveRDS(lasso_fit_1, filename)
} else {
  lasso_fit_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
lasso_fit_1
```


```{r}
broom::glance(lasso_fit_1)
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "lasso_lf_1.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  lasso_lf_1 <- lasso_wflow_1 %>%
    finalize_workflow(best_parameters) %>%
    last_fit(basic_split)
  saveRDS(lasso_lf_1, filename)
} else {
  lasso_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
}
#lasso_lf_1  # this will cause a large printout when knitting
```

```{r}
lasso_lf_1 %>% extract_workflow() %>% broom::glance()
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
lasso_lf_1 %>% collect_metrics()
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
lasso_perf_1 <- int_pctl(lasso_lf_1, times=10, metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Lasso regression 1")
lasso_perf_1
```

Effect sizes.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "lasso_cis_1.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  #logistic_cis_1 <- compute_cis(logistic_fit_1)
  lasso_cis_1 <- lasso_lf_1 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_parsnip() %>% tidy()  # exponentiate = TRUE does not work
  
  lasso_cis_1 <- lasso_cis_1 %>% 
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = exp(estimate),
      conf.low = estimate, conf.high = estimate)   # Fake confidence intervals for plot_cis function
  write_tsv(lasso_cis_1, filename)
} else {
  lasso_cis_1 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r}
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "lasso_cis_1.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(lasso_cis_1, "Lasso model 1", lasso=TRUE)
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
g
# lasso_cis_1 %>% filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>% 
#   ggplot(aes(estimate, term)) + 
#   geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color="gray") +
#   geom_point() +
#   labs(title="Lasso 1")
```


### Combine results

ranger object does not have a glance method.

```{r}
#broom::glance(logistic_fit_1)
map(list(logistic=logistic_fit_1, lasso=lasso_fit_1), broom::glance) %>%
  bind_rows(.id="method")
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
all_performances <- bind_rows(logistic_perf_1, rf_perf_1, lasso_perf_1)
all_performances
all_performances %>% ggplot(aes(xmin=.lower, xmax=.upper, x=.estimate, y=model)) +
  geom_pointrange() + facet_wrap(~ .metric)#, scales = "free_x")
```



```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "logistic_lf_1.rds")
logistic_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "lasso_lf_1.rds")
lasso_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "rf_lf_1.rds")
rf_lf_1 <- readRDS(filename)
```



#### ROC and PR curves



```{r}
preds <- #list(logistic=logistic_lf_1, lasso=lasso_lf_1, rf=rf_lf_1) %>% 
  list(logistic=logistic_fit_1, lasso=lasso_fit_1, rf=rf_fit_1) %>% 
  #map(collect_predictions) %>%
  map(function(x) augment(x, new_data=train)) %>%
  bind_rows(.id = "model") %>%
  group_by(model)
```


```{r}
preds %>%
  roc_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = "second") %>%
  autoplot() +
  labs(title="ROC model 1")
```

```{r}
preds %>%
  pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = "second") %>%
  autoplot() +
  labs(title="PR model 1")
```

#### Effect sizes

Random forest importance plot.

```{r}

rf_vip_1 <- rf_lf_1 %>%
  extract_workflow() %>%
  extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
  vi()
g2 <- rf_vip_1 %>%
  add_row(Variable = "H63D_x_C282Y", Importance=NA) %>%
  mutate(term = Variable, estimate = Importance,
         term = fct_relevel(term, intersect(names(pretty_conv), term)),
         term = fct_relabel(term, ~ coalesce(pretty_conv[.x], .x)),
         term = fct_rev(term)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=estimate, y=term)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title="Random forest 1", x="Importance", y="")
g2
```

Logistic regression versus lasso.

```{r}
cis_1 <- bind_rows(logistic = logistic_cis_1, 
                   lasso = lasso_cis_1, #) %>% 
                   #filter(term != "(Intercept)")# %>%
                   #mutate(estimate = exp(estimate)
                   #       ), 
                 .id = "model")
g1 <- cis_1 %>% 
  mutate(term = str_remove(term, "TRUE"),
         term = fct_relevel(term, intersect(names(pretty_conv), term)),
         #term = fct_recode(!!pretty_conv), 
         term = fct_relabel(term, ~ coalesce(pretty_conv[.x], .x)),
         term = fct_rev(term),
         hollow_color = if_else((model == "lasso" & estimate == 1) | 
                                  (model == "logistic" & conf.low < 1 & conf.high > 1), 
                                NA, 
                                model)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=estimate, xmin=conf.low, xmax=conf.high, y=term, color=model, fill=hollow_color)) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray") +
  geom_linerange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) + 
  geom_point(shape=21, size=3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  scale_fill_discrete(na.value=NA, guide="none") +  
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title="Model 1", x="Odds ratio", y="Variable") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#g <- g1 + g2
g <- g1
g
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "cis_1.pdf")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
```


### Confusion matrix

```{r}
confusion_matrix <- function(model, newdata=NULL) { # Unfinished
  pred <- augment(model, type.predict = "response", newdata=newdata)
  pred
}
# compute_helper <- function(model, newdata=NULL, boot.n=2000) {
#   pred <- augment(model, type.predict = "link", newdata=newdata)
#   g <- pred %>% ggplot(aes(.fitted)) + geom_histogram()
#   distribution_df = pred %>% select(hemochromatosis, .fitted) %>% summary()
#   res <- create_precision_recall_new(pred$hemochromatosis, pred$.fitted, boot.n=boot.n)
#   res$pr_plot
#   list(distribution=g, distribution_df=distribution_df, pr_plot=res$pr_plot, pr_ci=res$pr_ci)
# }
```






## Model 2

With HC credible snips and some diseases.

### Logistic regression

```{r}
variables2 <- c("blood_donor", "sex2", "age", disease_names, credible_snip_names, known_snip_names, hla_snip_names)
f2 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", 
             paste(variables2, collapse=" + ")
             )
f2
```

```{r}
logistic_mod_2 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_2
```


```{r}
logistic_recipe_2 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f2), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ C282Y:H63D)
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_2
```

```{r}
logistic_wflow_2 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_2) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_2)
logistic_wflow_2
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_2.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_2 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_2, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_2, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_2 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r}
logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow() #%>% extract_fit_parsnip()
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
class(logistic_lf_2)
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_2)
```




```{r}
logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r}
logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

All VIFs are below 5, except two hla snips. These two snips are just the ones that have the reference allele as minor allele.

Let's check the correlations.

```{r}
train %>% 
  select(all_of(hla_snip_names), all_of(credible_snip_names), all_of(known_snip_names)) %>% 
  my_correlation() %>% 
  plot_correlation() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust=1, hjust=1))
```

Lot's of LD in the HLA area, as expected.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% plot()
```

R2 value:

```{r}
logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_2.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_2 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_2 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_2, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_2 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_2.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_2, "Logistic model 2")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
g
```



## Old Lasso model

```{r, eval=FALSE}
f <- as.formula("hemochromatosis ~ blood_donor + sex + age")
x <- model.matrix(f, data = train)[,-1]
y <- train$hemochromatosis
# Use cross validation to find optimal lambda hyperparameter of lasso over a grid of candidate values
grid <- 10^seq(10, -2, length = 100)
train_indices <- sample(1:nrow(x), nrow(x) / 2)
lasso_mod <- cv.glmnet(x[train_indices, ], y[train_indices], family="binomial", alpha = 1, lambda = grid)
plot(lasso_mod)
best_lambda <- lasso_mod$lambda.min
cat(glue("Best lambda is {best_lambda}"))
```


## Model 3

```{r}
variables3 <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                credible_snip_names,
                "supersnip", "rs3094093", "rs114179634", "rs9261354")
f3 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables3, collapse=" + "))
f3
```

```{r logistic-mod-3}
logistic_mod_3 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_3
```


```{r logistic_recipe-3}
logistic_recipe_3 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f3), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ C282Y : c(H63D, S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post)) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_3
```


```{r logistic-workflow-3}
logistic_wflow_3 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_3) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_3)
logistic_wflow_3
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-3}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_3.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_3 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_3, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_3, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_3 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_3)
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_3)
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r logistic-perf-3, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_3 <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_3, times=10, 
                            metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 3")
logistic_perf_3
```

The below way of computing R2 does not work
```{r logistic-perf-3b, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_3b <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_3, times=10, 
                            metrics = yardstick::metric_set(rsq_trad)) %>%  # Neither does rsq work
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 3")
logistic_perf_3b
```

Show trained recipe.

```{r}
logistic_lf_3 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```

The step_lincomb removed zero variables.

Basic info about the results. Turn off pretty printing otherwise knitting will hang.

```{r basic_3, paged.print=FALSE}
#logistic_lf_3
```

Basic info about the fit.

```{r}
logistic_lf_3 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```


Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-3}
logistic_lf_3 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

C282Y and its interaction with H1_2 has very high VIF values!!!

R2 value:

```{r}
logistic_lf_3 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r logistic-cis-3}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_3.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_3 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_3 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_3, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_3 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-plot-3}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_3.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_3, "Logistic model 3")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
g
```


## Model 4

This is like model 3 but recessive model for snips C282Y and H1-2 is used.

```{r}
variables4 <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                credible_snip_names,
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634", "rs9261354")
f4 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables4, collapse=" + "))
f4
```

```{r logistic-mod-4}
logistic_mod_4 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_4
```


```{r logistic_recipe-4}
logistic_recipe_4 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f4), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.integer(C282Y == 2)) %>%     # Encode C282Y and H1-2 as recessive snips
              #H1_2  = as.integer(H1_2 == 2)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ C282Y : c(H63D, S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post)) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_4
```


```{r logistic-workflow-4}
logistic_wflow_4 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_4) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_4)
logistic_wflow_4
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-4}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_4.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_4 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_4, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_4, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_4 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_4)
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_4)
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r logistic-perf-4, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_4 <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_4, times=10, 
                            metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 4")
logistic_perf_4
```


Show trained recipe.

```{r}
logistic_lf_4 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```

The step_lincomb removed two interactions!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


Basic info about the fit.

```{r}
logistic_lf_4 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```


Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-4}
logistic_lf_4 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

C282Y and its interaction with H1_2 has very high VIF values!!!

R2 value.

```{r}
logistic_lf_4 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r logistic-cis-4}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_4.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_4 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_4 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_4, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_4 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-plot-4}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_4.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_4, "Logistic model 4")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g)
g
```




## Model 5

Best FinnGen logistic model

### Basic logistic model

This is like model 3 but full model for snips C282Y, H63D and ~H1-2~ is used.

```{r}
variables5 <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                credible_snip_names,
                disease_names,
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                )
f5 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables5, collapse=" + "))
f5
```

```{r logistic-mod-5}
logistic_mod_5 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_5
```


```{r logistic_recipe-5}
logistic_recipe_5_pre <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f5), data = train) %>%   # The data is needed here only for the names and types of columns
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  # step_mutate(C282Y = as.integer(C282Y == 2),     # Encode C282Y and H1-2 as recessive snips
  #             H1_2  = as.integer(H1_2 == 2)) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D))
              #H1_2  = as.factor(H1_2)) %>%
logistic_recipe_5 <- logistic_recipe_5_pre %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
                                                   #rs3094093, rs114179634,
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_5
```


```{r logistic-workflow-5}
logistic_wflow_5 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_5) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_5)
logistic_wflow_5
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-5}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_5 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_5, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_5)
```

```{r full-fit-5}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_full_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_full_5 <- fit(logistic_wflow_5, basic)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r}
logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% exact_zeros_or_ones()
logistic_full_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% exact_zeros_or_ones()
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_5)
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r logistic-perf-5, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_5 <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_5, times=10, 
                            metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 5")
logistic_perf_5
```


Show trained recipe.

```{r}
logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```

The step_lincomb removed two interactions!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


Basic info about the fit.

```{r glance5}
logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```

```{r}
logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-5}
logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

C282Y and its interaction with H1_2 has very high VIF values!!!

Calibration.

```{r}
logistic_lf_5_test_pred <- augment(logistic_lf_5)
logistic_lf_5_test_pred %>%
  cal_plot_windowed(truth = hemochromatosis, estimate = .pred_case, step_size = 0.025, event_level="second")
```

##### Forest plots

```{r logistic-cis-5}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_5 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_5 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```



```{r logistic-cis-plot-5}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_5.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_5, "Logistic model 5 FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_5)) + labs(y="Variable")
##ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-5}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_full_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_full_5 <- compute_cis(logistic_full_5)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-plot-5}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_5.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_full_5, "Logistic model 5 full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_full_5)) + labs(y="Variable")
#ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

The following computes the effect sizes of combinations of predictors with confidence intervals.

```{r}
fit <- logistic_full_5 %>% extract_fit_engine()

# The columns C282Y and H63D are not really needed in the below tibble
df <-  tribble(
    ~C282Y, ~H63D, ~`variables`,
    0, 0, c(),
    0, 1, c("H63D_X1"),
    0, 2, c("H63D_X2"),
    1, 0, c("C282Y_X1"),
    1, 1, c("C282Y_X1", "H63D_X1", "C282Y_X1_x_H63D_X1"),
    1, 2, c("C282Y_X1", "H63D_X2", "C282Y_X1_x_H63D_X2"),
    2, 0, c("C282Y_X2"),
    2, 1, c("C282Y_X2", "H63D_X1", "C282Y_X2_x_H63D_X1"),
    2, 2, c("C282Y_X2", "H63D_X2", "C282Y_X2_x_H63D_X2"),
  )
  #summarise(C282Y, H63D, get_odds_ratios(fit, variables, exponentiate = TRUE))
cis <- get_odds_ratio_sums(fit, df, exponentiate=TRUE)
cis %>% 
  mutate(name = glue("C282Y={C282Y} H63D={H63D}"),
         name = fct_rev(name)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=estimate, y = name, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray") +
  geom_point() +
  geom_linerange() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x="Odds ratio", y="Dosage combination")
```


#### Risk table

Create synthetic data.

```{r}
# The value of age is stratified by sex
mean_ages5 <- train %>% group_by(sex2) %>% summarise(age = mean(age))
data5 <- expand_grid(sex2 = c("male", "pre", "post"), donations_in_five_years=seq(0, 10, 5),  
                    credible1=0:2, credible2=0, credible3=0,
                    I9_HYPTENSESS=FALSE, M13_ARTHROSIS=FALSE, K11_CHOLELITH=FALSE, E4_HYTHYNAS=FALSE, C3_CANCER=FALSE,
                    H63D=0:2, S65C=0, C282Y=0:2, supersnip=0:2, 
                    rs3094093=2, rs114179634=0) %>% 
  mutate(across(sex2, as.factor))
# data5 <- data5 %>% mutate(age=mean(train$age))     # This was a bad idea
data5 <- data5 %>% inner_join(mean_ages5, by="sex2")
important_predictors5 <- c(Donations="donations_in_five_years", Sex2="sex2", "H63D", "C282Y", HLA3="supersnip", Causal1="credible1") %>% fill_names()

cat("The following variables were fixed during the computation of probabilities\n")
fixed_predictors5 <- get_fixed_predictors(data5, important_predictors5)
fixed_predictors5
```


New version


New version, train data.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=12}
title <- "Model 5 train data"
```
