```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=12}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_5_train.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) | recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = train, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = train, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors5, title=title, col = "sex2", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_5_train.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=12, title=title)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
```

New version, test data.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=12}
title <- "Model 5 test data"
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_5_test.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) | recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = test, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = test, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors5, title=title, col = "sex2", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_5_test.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=12, title=title)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)

```

full data.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=12}
title <- "Model 5 full data"
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_5_full.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) | recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_full_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = basic, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_full_5, data5, important_predictors5, count_data = basic, col="sex2", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors5, title=title, col = "sex2", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80),
                     number_of_blocks = 6)
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_5_full.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=12, title=title)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)

```

Try with maximum allowed donations.

18-25-vuotiaille naisille suosittelemme luovuttamista korkeintaan kerran vuodessa ja muille naisille enintään 2-3 kertaa vuodessa.

Miehille suosittelemme enintään 3-4 verenluovutusta vuodessa.

Sallitut minimivälit kokoverenluovutuksille ovat naisille 91 ja miehille 61 vuorokautta. 

```{r}
# The value of age is stratified by sex
mean_ages5 <- train %>% group_by(sex2) %>% summarise(age = mean(age))
mean_ages5
```


```{r}
maximum_donations <- tibble(sex2 = fct_inorder(c("male", "pre", "post")),
                            donations_in_five_years = c(5 * 4, 5 * 3, 5 * 3))
maximum_donations
```


```{r}
data5b <- expand_grid(sex2 = c("male", "pre", "post"),
                    credible1=0:2, credible2=0, credible3=0,
                    I9_HYPTENSESS=FALSE, M13_ARTHROSIS=FALSE, K11_CHOLELITH=FALSE, E4_HYTHYNAS=FALSE, C3_CANCER=FALSE,
                    H63D=0, S65C=0, C282Y=2, supersnip=0:2, 
                    rs3094093=2, rs114179634=0) %>% 
  mutate(across(sex2, as.factor))
data5b <- data5b %>% 
  inner_join(mean_ages5, by="sex2") %>%
  inner_join(maximum_donations, by="sex2")
important_predictors5b <- c(Donations="donations_in_five_years", Sex2="sex2", "H63D", "C282Y", HLA3="supersnip", Causal1="credible1") %>% fill_names()

cat("The following variables were fixed during the computation of probabilities\n")
fixed_predictors5b <- get_fixed_predictors(data5, important_predictors5)
fixed_predictors5b
```

```{r}
cis <- compute_risk_cis(logistic_full_5, data5b, important_predictors5b, count_data = basic, conf.level = 0.95)
cis %>% group_by(sex2) %>% filter(n >= 5, credible1 == 0, supersnip == 1) %>% slice_max(n=3, order_by=mean) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean, lower, upper), ~ sprintf("%.3f", .)))
```

Combined effect.

```{r}
C282Y_plus_HLA3(logistic_cis_full_5)
```

### EOF age model

Replace event age by EOF age. This is not needed anymore since all logistic models using EOF age by default

```{r, eval=FALSE}
basic2 <- basic %>% 
  select(-(int1:int67)) %>%
  left_join(EOF_AGE, by="sample") %>%
  mutate(age = AGE_AT_DEATH_OR_END_OF_FOLLOWUP,
         sex2 = create_sex2(sex, age))   # We need to recreate this
```



```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_eof_age_full_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_eof_age_full_5 <- fit(logistic_wflow_5, basic2)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_eof_age_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_eof_age_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5 <- compute_cis(logistic_eof_age_full_5)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
age_comparison <- bind_rows(event_age = logistic_cis_full_5, eof_age = logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5, .id="model")
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
#| fig-cap: The event age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls in the event age model. The event age is the age at the end of followup in the eof_age model.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_eof_age_full_5.pdf")
g <- plot_cis2(age_comparison, "Logistic model eof age 5 full FinnGen") + labs(y="Variable")
g <- g + guides(color = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE, override.aes = list(shape = 16)))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

### Alternative hemochromatosis definition

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "venesections.tsv.gz")
venesections <- read_tsv(filename)
venesections <- venesections %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor))
venesections %>% select(-FINNGENID) %>% summary()
```

```{r}
venesection_count <- venesections %>%
  count(FINNGENID, name = "count")
```

Form new endpoint.

```{r}
basic3 <- basic %>%
  select(-(int1:int67)) %>%
  left_join(venesection_count, by=c(sample="FINNGENID")) %>%
  replace_na(list(count=0)) %>%
  mutate(new_hemochromatosis = case_when(hemochromatosis == "case" & count > 0  ~ "case",
                                         hemochromatosis == "case" & count == 0 ~ NA,
                                         TRUE                                   ~ "control"
                                       ) %>% 
           factor(levels = c("control", "case")))
basic3 %>% select(hemochromatosis, new_hemochromatosis) %>% table(useNA = "always")  
basic2 <- basic3 %>% mutate(hemochromatosis=new_hemochromatosis) %>% filter(!is.na(hemochromatosis))
rm(basic3)
```

Drops S65C and its interactions.

```{r}
alternative_variables5 <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", 
                "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                credible_snip_names,
                disease_names,
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                )
alternative_f5 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(alternative_variables5, collapse=" + "))
alternative_f5
```

```{r}
logistic_alternative_mod_5 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_alternative_mod_5
```


```{r}
logistic_alternative_recipe_5 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(alternative_f5), data = train) %>%   # The data is needed here only for the names and types of columns
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  # step_mutate(C282Y = as.integer(C282Y == 2),     # Encode C282Y and H1-2 as recessive snips
  #             H1_2  = as.integer(H1_2 == 2)) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
              #H1_2  = as.factor(H1_2)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
                                                   #rs3094093, rs114179634,
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_alternative_recipe_5
```


```{r}
logistic_alternative_wflow_5 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_alternative_mod_5) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_alternative_recipe_5)
logistic_alternative_wflow_5
```

This does not fit well, use Firth regression instead.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_alternative_full_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_alternative_full_5 <- fit(logistic_alternative_wflow_5, 
                                 basic2)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_alternative_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_alternative_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
logistic_alternative_full_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% summary()
```

The following produces lots of warnings:
Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred

Variance inflation factors.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_alternative_full_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```


```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_alternative_full_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_alternative_full_5 <- compute_cis(logistic_alternative_full_5)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_alternative_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_alternative_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```
Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurredWarning: collapsing to unique 'x' valuesWarning: collapsing to unique 'x' values

```{r, eval=FALSE}
#filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_5.pdf")
g <- logistic_cis_alternative_full_5 %>% 
  # Filter out variables whose confidence intervals were really large
  filter(! (conf.high > 1e50 | conf.low < 1e-50)) %>%
  # filter(! term %in% c("donations_in_five_years", "S65C", "sex2_pre", "C282Y_X1_x_S65C", "C282Y_X1_x_donations_in_five_years",
  #                      "C282Y_X1_x_sex2_pre", "C282Y_X2_x_donations_in_five_years", "C282Y_X2_x_sex2_pre")) %>%
  plot_cis("Logistic model 5 alternative full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_alternative_full_5)) + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_alternative_full_5.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```


#### Firth's logistic regression.

```{r}
baked <- logistic_alternative_full_5 %>% extract_recipe() %>% bake(new_data = basic2)
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "firth_alternative_full_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  firth_alternative_full_5 <- logistf(formula = hemochromatosis ~ ., data = baked)
  saveRDS(firth_alternative_full_5, filename)
} else {
  firth_alternative_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r}
# Tidy the firth fit object
# I skip the chi2 statistic since it is a slightly more complicated to compute.
# See logistf:::summary.logistf
tidy_firth <- function(firth_fit) {
  df <- tibble(term = firth_fit$term, 
               estimate = exp(firth_fit$coefficients),
               std.error = diag(firth_fit$var)^0.5, 
               statistic = NA, 
               p.value = firth_fit$prob,
               conf.low = exp(firth_fit$ci.lower),
               conf.high=exp(firth_fit$ci.upper)) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)")
  df
}
```


```{r}
#capt <- capture.output(s <- summary(firth)) # Summary also prints the results, here we discard it.
# I skip the chi2 statistic since it is a slightly more complicated to compute.
# See logistf:::summary.logistf
filename <- file.path(table_path, "firth_cis_alternative_full_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  firth_cis_alternative_full_5 <- tidy_firth(firth_alternative_full_5)
  write_tsv(firth_cis_alternative_full_5, filename)
} else {
  firth_cis_alternative_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
g <- firth_cis_alternative_full_5 %>% 
  plot_cis("Firth model 5 alternative full FinnGen") + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "firth_cis_alternative_full_5.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```

#### Combine forest plot

```{r}
df <- bind_rows(normal = logistic_cis_full_5, 
                alternative = logistic_cis_alternative_full_5 %>% 
                  # Filter out variables whose confidence intervals were really large
                  filter(! (conf.high > 1e50 | conf.low < 1e-50)),
                alternative_firth=firth_cis_alternative_full_5, .id="model") %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels=c("normal", "alternative", "alternative_firth")))
g <- df %>%
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_cis2("Alternative logistic 5 full FinnGen") +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse=TRUE))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "comparison_cis_alternative_full_5.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```

### Binned model

This was pointless exercise.

```{r logistic-mod-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
logistic_mod_5_binned <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_5_binned
```


```{r logistic_recipe-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
#donation_breaks <- c(0,5,10,15,20,25)
donation_breaks <- c(0,5,15,25)
logistic_recipe_5_binned <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f5), data = train) %>% 
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_mutate(donation_bin = cut(donations_in_five_years, breaks = donation_breaks, right=FALSE)) %>%
  step_rm(donations_in_five_years) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D, donation_bin) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, #starts_with("donation_bin"), 
                                                   sex2_pre, sex2_post
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
logistic_recipe_5_binned
```


```{r logistic-workflow-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
logistic_wflow_5_binned <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_5_binned) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_5_binned)
logistic_wflow_5_binned
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-5-bin, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_5_binned.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_5_binned <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_5_binned, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_5_binned, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_5_binned <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_5_binned)
```

```{r full-fit-5-bin, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_full_5_binned.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_full_5_binned <- fit(logistic_wflow_5_binned, basic)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_full_5_binned, filename)
} else {
  logistic_full_5_binned <- readRDS(filename)
}
```






Show trained recipe.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_binned %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```

The step_lincomb removed two interactions!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!


Basic info about the fit.

```{r glance5-binned, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_binned %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_binned %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_binned %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

C282Y and its interaction with H1_2 has very high VIF values!!!



```{r logistic-cis-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_5_binned.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_5_binned <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_5_binned %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_5_binned, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_5_binned <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-plot-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_5.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_5, "Logistic model 5 FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_5)) + labs(y="Variable")
##ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_full_5.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_full_5 <- compute_cis(logistic_full_5)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_full_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-plot-5-binned, eval=FALSE}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_5.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_full_5, "Logistic model 5 full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_full_5)) + labs(y="Variable")
#ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

### Model 5 C282Y wild only

```{r}
variables5_wild <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                "H63D", "S65C", 
                "C282Y", 
                "H1_2",
                "supersnip",
                #credible_snip_names,
                disease_names,
                "credible1",
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                )
f5_wild <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables5_wild, collapse=" + "))
f5_wild
```

```{r logistic-mod-5-wild}
logistic_mod_5 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_5
```


```{r logistic_recipe-5-wild}
my_dummy_names <- function(var, lvl, ordinal=FALSE, sep="_") {
  conv <- list(`0`="ref", `1`="hetero", `2`="homo")
  if (ordinal) stop("Not implemented")
  paste(var, conv[as.character(lvl)] %>% unlist() %>% unname(), sep = sep)
}
logistic_recipe_5_wild <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f5_wild), data = train) %>%
  step_filter(C282Y == 0) %>%   # non-carriers only
  step_rm(C282Y) %>%
  #step_rm(H1_2) %>%
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_mutate(#C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D), #) %>%
              H1_2  = as.factor(H1_2)
              ) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), H63D) %>%
  # step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
  #                                                  #rs3094093, rs114179634,
  #                                                  )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
#  step_interact(terms = ~ H63D:C282Y + H1_2:C282Y + S65C:C282Y + supersnips:C282Y)
logistic_recipe_5_wild
```


```{r logistic-workflow-5-wild}
logistic_wflow_5_wild <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_5) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_5_wild)
logistic_wflow_5_wild
```



This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-5-wild}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_5_wild.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_5_wild <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_5_wild, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_5_wild, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_5_wild <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_5_wild)
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_5_wild)
```

Use Dharma to check some diagnostics.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
simulated_5_wild <- DHARMa::simulateResiduals(logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_fit_engine())
plot(simulated_5_wild)
```

I don't know how to interpret the right side panel, but it doesn't look like it should. Why is there no messages printed?

Let's check the diagnostics with the performance package.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_5_wild.check_model.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename)) {
  res <- logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% performance::check_model()
  saveRDS(res, filename)
} else {
  res <- readRDS(filename)
}
res
```

The binned residuals look terrible. Let's try to plot them per each predictor.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% performance::binned_residuals(term = "age") %>% plot()
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% performance::binned_residuals(term = "donations_in_five_years") %>% plot()
```

Confidence intervals for the performance measures using percentile method.

```{r logistic-perf-5-wild, eval=FALSE}
# Use 10 bootstrap samples
logistic_perf_5_wild <- int_pctl(logistic_lf_5_wild, times=10, 
                            metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, brier_class, roc_auc, pr_auc)) %>% 
  mutate(model="Logistic regression 5 wild")
logistic_perf_5_wild
```


Show trained recipe.

```{r}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe()
```



Basic info about the fit.

```{r glance5_wild}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% glance()
```


Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-5-wild}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```


R2 value.

```{r}
logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r logistic-cis-5-wild}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_5_wild.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_5_wild <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_5_wild, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_5_wild <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```



Computing confidence intervals resulted in warnings: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred.

What to do in these situations?
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67360883/how-to-fix-fitted-probabilities-numerically-0-or-1-occurred-warning-in-r

Check if there are separation issues.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
my_fit <- function(recipe, data, scale = FALSE, method = "detect_separation") {
  # method could be "detect_separation" or "glm.fit"
  preprocessed <- recipe %>% bake(data)
  if (scale)
    preprocessed <- preprocessed %>% mutate(across(-hemochromatosis, scale))
  #preprocessed <- preprocessed %>% mutate(hemochromatosis = as.numeric(hemochromatosis=="case"))
  fit <- glm(hemochromatosis ~ ., data = preprocessed, family = binomial, method=method)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "detect_separation_5_wild.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename)) {
  fit_separation <- my_fit(logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe(), train)
  saveRDS(fit_separation, filename)
} else {
  fit_separation <- readRDS(filename)
}
fit_separation
```

Checking that this produces the same result as with tidymodels.

Note: apparently bake() does not perform step step_filter(), unless skip parameter is set to FALSE.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
fit <- my_fit(logistic_lf_5_wild %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_recipe(), train %>% filter(C282Y==0), method="glm.fit")
cis <- compute_cis(fit)
plot_cis(cis,
         "Logistic model 5 wild (lm)", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_5_wild)) + labs(y="Variable")
```

No separation detected. Scaling variables did not help. Dharma results were hard to interpret.
Maybe confidence intervals can be computed using some other method, such as parameters::parameters. But that does not compile currently in
CRAN.

```{r logistic-cis-wild-plot-5}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_5_wild.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_5_wild, "Logistic model 5 wild FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_5_wild)) + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs) {
  ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_5_wild.pdf"), 
         g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```

### Model 5 XGboost



```{r}
# These hyperparameters should be optimised!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
xg_mod_5 <- boost_tree(learn_rate = 0.1, trees = 100) |>
  set_mode("classification") |> 
  set_engine("xgboost", verbose = 0)
```


```{r}
xg_recipe_5 <- #logistic_recipe_5_pre %>%
  recipe(as.formula(f5), data = xg_train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_integer(all_nominal_predictors(), all_of(disease_names), zero_based=TRUE) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
xg_recipe_5
```

From
https://modeloriented.github.io/shapviz/articles/tidymodels.html
Remark: Dont use 1:m transforms such as One-Hot-Encodings. They are usually not necessary and make the workflow more complicated. If you cant avoid this, check the collapse argument in shapviz().

```{r xg-workflow-5}
xg_wflow_5 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(xg_mod_5) %>% 
  add_recipe(xg_recipe_5)
xg_wflow_5
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "xg_fit_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  tic("Fitting XGboost model")
  xg_fit_5 <- xg_wflow_5 %>% fit(xg_train)
  saveRDS(xg_fit_5, filename)
  toc()
} else {
  xg_fit_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(xg_fit_5)
```

Took 30 seconds.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
xg_fit_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% fmsb::NagelkerkeR2()
```

```{r}
xg_fit_5 %>% extract_recipe()
```

#### Performance measures

Note that the "positive" level needs to be specified using the event_level option. The default is the first level, which is control in our case,
which is not what we want.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
#xg_fit_5 %>% augment(new_data=xg_train) %>% pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = "first") %>% autoplot()
```

```{r}
res <- performance_curves(xg_fit_5, "xgboost 5 train", new_data = xg_train, event_level = "first")
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```



Cases as positive event.

```{r}
xg_perf1 <- xg_fit_5 %>% augment(new_data=xg_train) %>% mymetrics_rev(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class)

logistic_perf1 <- logistic_lf_5 %>% 
  extract_workflow() %>%
  augment(new_data=train) %>% #mutate(hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))) %>% 
  mymetrics(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class)
bind_rows(xg=xg_perf1, logistic=logistic_perf1, .id = "model") %>% pivot_wider(names_from=model, values_from=.estimate) %>% select(-.estimator)
```

  
As a sanity check, try with control as positive events.

```{r}
xg_perf2 <- xg_fit_5 %>% augment(new_data=xg_train) %>% mymetrics_rev(hemochromatosis, .pred_control, estimate=.pred_class)

logistic_perf2 <- logistic_lf_5 %>%
  extract_workflow() %>%
  augment(new_data=train) %>% #mutate(hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))) %>% 
  mymetrics(hemochromatosis, .pred_control, estimate=.pred_class)
bind_rows(xg=xg_perf2, logistic=logistic_perf2, .id = "model") %>% pivot_wider(names_from=model, values_from=.estimate) %>% select(-.estimator)
```

Obtain more accurate performance measures using cross validation.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_resamples_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  tic("Resampling for logistic 5")
  logistic_resamples_5 <- 
    logistic_wflow_5 %>% 
    fit_resamples(folds, metrics = mymetrics, control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE, event_level="second"))
  toc()
  saveRDS(logistic_resamples_5, filename)
} else {
  logistic_resamples_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
collect_metrics(logistic_resamples_5)
```

Note that the below AUROC and  AUPR values are slightly different than above. This is because below we have formed the two curves
based on out-of-sample predictions, whereas above we have taken the average of the areas under the ten curves.

```{r}
res <- performance_curves(logistic_resamples_5, "logistic 5 CV")
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "xg_resamples_5.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  xg_resamples_5 <- 
    xg_wflow_5 %>% 
    fit_resamples(xg_folds, metrics = mymetrics_rev, control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE, event_level="first"))
  saveRDS(xg_resamples_5, filename)
} else {
  xg_resamples_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
collect_metrics(xg_resamples_5)
```

```{r}
res <- performance_curves(xg_resamples_5, "xgboost 5 CV", event_level="first")
res[[1]] + res[[2]]
```

The performance measures as a plot.

```{r}
performance_measures <- list(logistic = collect_metrics(logistic_resamples_5), xg = collect_metrics(xg_resamples_5)) %>%
  bind_rows(.id = "model")
```

```{r}
performance_measures %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=mean, xmin=mean-std_err, xmax=mean+std_err, y=.metric, color=model)) + 
  geom_pointrange(position=position_dodge(width=0.3)) +
  labs(y="Metric", x="Mean", title="Performance")
```

Huge difference in brier classification metric between the two models!

#### SHAP analysis

```{r}
set.seed(43)
xg_explain_5 <- xg_train %>% 
  #head(n=1000) %>% 
  slice_sample(n=10000) #%>%
  #mutate(hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))
X_pred <- bake(  # goes to xgboost:::predict.xgb.Booster()
  xg_fit_5 %>% extract_recipe(), 
  has_role("predictor"),
  new_data = xg_explain_5,
  composition = "matrix"
)

#stopifnot(colnames(X_pred) %in% colnames(xg_explain_5))
```

Compute the SHAP values.

```{r}
# Note, the which_class parameter works only for multiclass classification, not for binary classification.
# shapviz assumes that the first level is the positive class. That cannot be changed, so we have
# to accept, that the directions of effects are opposite here.
tic("Computing shap values")
set.seed(158)
shap_values <- xg_fit_5 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>%
  shapviz(X_pred = X_pred, X = xg_explain_5, which_class=1, interactions = TRUE) #|> 
  #setNames(levels(iris$Species))
toc()
```



```{r}
shap_values %>% 
  sv_importance() +
  ggtitle("SHAP importance")
```

Store importances to file.

```{r}
xg_importance_5 <- shap_values %>% sv_importance(kind="no") %>% enframe()
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "xg_importance_5.tsv")
  write_tsv(xg_importance_5, filename)
}
xg_importance_5
```

```{r}
shap_values |> 
  sv_importance(kind = "bee") +
  ggtitle("SHAP importance")
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence(v = "C282Y", color_var = "age") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 1, guides = "collect")
```


```{r}
xvars <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years" , "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", "supersnip", "credible1", "credible2", "credible3", "rs3094093", "rs114179634", disease_names)
shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence(xvars, color_var = NULL) +
  plot_annotation("SHAP importance")
  #plot_layout(ncol = 1, guides = "collect")
```

Dependence. The variable that is mapped to colors is the one with strongest interaction with the feature in question.

```{r, fig.width=10}
shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence(xvars, color_var = "auto") +
  plot_annotation("Dependence")
```

Pure interactions.

```{r, fig.width=10}
shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence(xvars, color_var = "auto", interactions = TRUE) +
  plot_annotation("Pure interactions")
```

Let's look more closely at the interactions of "donations in five years".

```{r, fig.width=10}
shap_values |> 
  sv_dependence("donations_in_five_years", color_var = xvars, interactions = TRUE) +
  plot_annotation("Pure interactions of donations in five years")
```


### Bayesian model 5

```{r logistic-mod-5b}
#logistic_mod_5b <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_5b <- bayesian::bayesian(family="bernoulli", chains=4, cores=4, iter=2000) %>%  # This needs to be bernoulli, not binomial
  set_engine("brms") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
logistic_mod_5b
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
logistic_recipe_5b <- logistic_recipe_5 %>%
  step_mutate(hemochromatosis = if_else(hemochromatosis=="case", 1, 0)) # brms requires numeric response
```


```{r logistic-workflow-5b}
logistic_wflow_5b <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_5b) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_5)
logistic_wflow_5b
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-5b}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_5b.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute_bayes) {
  tic("Fit Bayesian model")
  logistic_lf_5b <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_5b, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  toc()
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_5b, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_5b <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_5b)
```

Got warnings/errors:

→ A | error:   Assigned data `orig_rows` must be compatible with existing data.
               ✖ Existing data has 824180 rows.
               ✖ Assigned data has 206045 rows.
               ℹ Only vectors of size 1 are recycled.
               Caused by error in `vectbl_recycle_rhs_rows()`:
               ! Can't recycle input of size 206045 to size 824180.
There were issues with some computations   A: x1
Warning: All models failed. Run `show_notes(.Last.tune.result)` for more information.

Fit Bayesian model: 1196.146 sec elapsed

```{r, eval=FALSE}
p <- logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_workflow() %>% augment(new_data = data5_test)
baked <- logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_recipe() %>% bake(data5_test)
p <- logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% augment()
p2 <- logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% augment(newdata = baked)   # This produces labels
x <- logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% posterior_epred(newdata = baked, summary=FALSE)  # This gives probabilities

```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_5b)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

extract_variables_names <- function(fit) {
  a <- as.array(fit)
  v <- attr(a, "dimnames")$variable
  return(setdiff(v, c("b_Intercept", "lp__")))
}
forest_plot <- function(fit, id, save_figs=FALSE) {
  bvariables <- extract_variables_names(fit)
  p <- mcmc_plot(fit, 
                 type = "areas",
                 prob = 0.95,
                 variable = bvariables,
                 area_method = "equal area",
                 transformations = "exp"
  ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color = "grey") +
    labs(x="Odds ratio") + 
    scale_x_log10()
  if (save_figs) {
    filename <- sprintf("%s/bayesian_area_plots_%s.png", fig_path, id)
    ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=p, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
  }
  p
}

# This self-made version has pretty labels unlike the previous function
forest_plot2 <- function(fit, id, descript, scale=1, variables=NULL) {
  #scale <- 3000
  #scale <- 0.9
  #bvariables = if (id=="all") bvariables else setdiff(bvariables, "b_femaleTRUE")
  if (is.null(variables)) {
    bvariables <- extract_variables_names(fit)
  } else {
    bvariables <- paste("b", variables, sep="_")
  }
  a <- as.array(fit, variable=bvariables)
  tmp <- bayesplot::mcmc_areas_data(a, 
                         prob = 0.95,
                         area_method = "equal area",
                         #variable = variables2,
                         transformations = "exp"
  )
  
  tmp2 <- tmp %>% 
    filter(parameter != "exp(b_intercept)") %>% 
    filter(parameter != "exp(Intercept)") %>% 
    filter(parameter != "exp(lprior)") %>% 
    mutate(name = str_match(parameter, "exp\\(b_(.*)\\)")[,2]) %>%
    left_join(descript, by="name") %>%
    mutate(Pretty = ifelse(is.na(value), name, value)) %>%
    mutate(Pretty = as.factor(Pretty))
  g2 <- tmp2 %>% 
    mutate(Pretty = fct_rev(Pretty)) %>%
    filter(interval == "inner") %>% 
    #filter(str_detect(parameter, r"(exp\(b_.*\))")) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x=x, y=Pretty, height=plotting_density)) + 
    ggridges::geom_ridgeline(scale=scale) +
    ggridges::geom_ridgeline(mapping=aes(x=x, y=Pretty, height=plotting_density), 
                   data=tmp2 %>% filter(interval=="outer"), 
                   fill=NA, scale=scale) +
    scale_y_discrete() +
    labs(x="Odds ratio", y="Variable") +
    scale_x_log10()
  g2
}

```

```{r}
forest_plot(logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine(), "sika", save_figs = FALSE)
```

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
g <- forest_plot2(logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine(), "sika", pretty_conv %>% enframe(), scale=3000)
g2 <- g + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray") +
  geom_pointrange(data=logistic_cis_5 %>% mutate(Pretty = coalesce(pretty_conv[term], term)), 
                  mapping=aes(x=estimate, xmin=conf.low, xmax=conf.high, y=Pretty), inherit.aes = FALSE, color="red")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_5_combined.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g2, width=10, height=7)
}
g2
```

```{r}
g <- forest_plot2(logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_fit_engine(), "sika", pretty_conv %>% enframe(), scale=1,
                  variables=c("rs3094093", "rs114179634"))#, "rs9261354"))
g + geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray")
```

Show just 95% intervals instead of distributions.

```{r logistic-cis-5b}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_5b.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_5b <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_5b %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_5b, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_5b <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r}
plot_double_cis <- function(cis, title) {
  cis %>% 
    mutate(term = str_remove(term, "TRUE"),
           term = fct_relevel(term, intersect(names(pretty_conv), term)),
           #term = fct_recode(!!pretty_conv), 
           term = fct_relabel(term, ~ coalesce(pretty_conv[.x], .x)),
           term = fct_rev(term),
           color = method,
           hollow_color = if_else(conf.low < 1 & conf.high > 1, NA, method)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x=estimate, xmin=conf.low, xmax=conf.high, y=term, color=color, fill=hollow_color)) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color="gray") +
    geom_linerange(position = position_dodge(1)) + 
    geom_point(shape=21, size=3, position = position_dodge(1)) +
    scale_fill_discrete(na.value=NA, guide="none") +  
    scale_x_log10() +
    labs(title=title, x="Odds ratio", y="Variable")
}
```


```{r}
g <- bind_rows(Frequentist=logistic_cis_5, Bayesian=logistic_cis_5b, .id = "method") %>%
  plot_double_cis("Logistic model 5 frequentist and Bayesian") +
  labs(color="Method")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_5_combined2.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=10, height=7)
}
g
```

### Cox regression

Time-dependent covariates model.

```{r}
#starts_with("C282Y") : c(starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
baseline_variables5 <- c("H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                        "supersnip",
                        credible_snip_names,
                        "rs3094093", "rs114179634")
interaction_variables5 <-  "C282Y:(H63D + S65C + supersnip + sex2 + donations_in_five_years)"
time_dependent_variables <- c("sex2", "donations_in_five_years", disease_names)
variables5_cox_tdc <- c(baseline_variables5, interaction_variables5, time_dependent_variables)
# f5_cox <- as.formula(sprintf("Surv(age, hemochromatosis) ~ %s", paste(variables5_cox_tdc, collapse=" + ")))
# f5_cox
#variables5_cox_tdc <- c(baseline_variables5, "donations_in_five_years", "sex2")
f5_cox_tdc <- as.formula(sprintf("Surv(tstart, tstop, status) ~ %s", paste(variables5_cox_tdc, collapse=" + ")))
f5_cox_tdc

```


Get ages at previous disease events.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "prior_endpoint_events_new.tsv.gz")
first_disease_ages <- read_tsv(filename, col_select = c(sample="FINNGENID", age="EVENT_AGE", endpoint="ENDPOINT"))
first_disease_ages <- first_disease_ages %>%
  group_by(sample, endpoint) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = age, n=1) %>%
  ungroup()
```

The time intervals are half-open. If the value of some covariate changes at time t1, then the value is valid
in the interval (t1,t2], where t2 is the next timepoint.

```{r}
# Called for every person separately
dynamic_five_year_donations <- function(donation_ages) {
  stopifnot(is.numeric(donation_ages))
  # The value of donations_in_five_years changes at blood donation and five years after blood donation
  ages <- c(donation_ages, donation_ages+5) %>% unique() %>% sort()
  res <- tibble(age=ages) %>%
    mutate(next_age = lead(age, default=1000)) %>%
    filter(! near(age, next_age))    # Fitting does not work if time intervals are too short
  res <- res <- res %>%
    mutate(donations_in_five_years = 
             map_int(age, 
                     function(age) {sum(((age - 5) < donation_ages) & (donation_ages <= age) ) })
           )
  res
}
```


```{r}
# Forms data for time-dependent covariate survival model
form_survival_data <- function(data) 
{
    # Baseline covariates and response
  tdc1 <- tmerge(data1 = data %>% select(sample, all_of(baseline_variables5)) %>%     # baseline covariates
                   mutate(across(c(C282Y, H63D), as.factor)),
                 data2 = data %>% select(sample, hemochromatosis, age) %>%            # response
                   mutate(hemochromatosis = as.integer(hemochromatosis=="case")),
                 id=sample, tstop=age, status = event(age, hemochromatosis))
  ## Add time-dependent variables
  # Donation count
  #dons <- donations %>% #arrange(APPROX_DONATION_DATE) %>% group_by(sample) %>% mutate(i = 1:n()) %>% ungroup() %>%
  #  mutate(sex2 = create_sex2(SEX, donation_age))
  
  # Add diseases
  tdc2 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc1,
                 data2 = first_disease_ages %>% filter(endpoint == "C3_CANCER") %>% select(sample, age),
                 id = sample,
                 C3_CANCER = tdc(age),
                 options = list(delay = 5))

  tdc2 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
                 data2 = first_disease_ages %>% filter(endpoint == "E4_HYTHYNAS") %>% select(sample, age),
                 id = sample,
                 E4_HYTHYNAS = tdc(age),
                 options = list(delay = 5))

  tdc2 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
                 data2 = first_disease_ages %>% filter(endpoint == "I9_HYPTENSESS") %>% select(sample, age),
                 id = sample,
                 I9_HYPTENSESS = tdc(age),
                 options = list(delay = 1))
  
  tdc2 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
                 data2 = first_disease_ages %>% filter(endpoint == "K11_CHOLELITH") %>% select(sample, age),
                 id = sample,
                 K11_CHOLELITH = tdc(age),
                 options = list(delay = 5))
  
  tdc2 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
                 data2 = first_disease_ages %>% filter(endpoint == "M13_ARTHROSIS") %>% select(sample, age),
                 id = sample,
                 M13_ARTHROSIS = tdc(age),
                 options = list(delay = 1))
  
  # Add variables premenopausal and postmenopausal
  females <- data %>% select(sample, sex) %>% filter(sex == "female")
  df <- tibble(age=c(0, 50), sex2=c("pre", "post"))
  sex2 <- females %>% mutate(df = list(df)) %>% unnest(df)
  tdc3 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
                 data2 = sex2,
                 id = sample,
                 sex2 = tdc(age, sex2, "male"))  # tdc(age, value, init)
  # postmenopausal <- females %>% mutate(age = 50)
  # tdc3 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc2,
  #                data2 = postmenopausal,
  #                id = sample,
  #                post = tdc(age))
  # 
  # df <- tibble(age=c(0, 50), premenopausal=c(1, 0))
  # premenopausal <- females %>% mutate(df = list(df)) %>% unnest(df)
  # tdc3 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc3,
  #                data2 = premenopausal,
  #                id = sample,
  #                pre = tdc(age, premenopausal, 0))  # tdc(age, value, init)
  
  # Add the donations in five years

  # Nest donation ages as a list
  don <- donations %>% select(sample, donation_age) %>% group_by(sample) %>% summarise(donation_ages = list(donation_age))
  
  # Nest time window endpoints as a list
  #ages <- tdc3 %>% select(sample, tstop) %>% group_by(sample) %>% summarise(ages = list(tstop))
  
  #df <- inner_join(don, ages, by="sample")
  donations_in_five_years <- don %>% #head(1000) %>% 
    rowwise() %>% 
    mutate(tmp = list(dynamic_five_year_donations(donation_ages))) %>% 
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-c(donation_ages)) %>% 
    unnest(tmp)

  tdc4 <- tmerge(data1 = tdc3,
                 data2 = donations_in_five_years,
                 id = sample,
                 donations_in_five_years = tdc(age, donations_in_five_years, 0))
  
  tdc4 
  # Below is the old version.
  
  # Note that this current implementation is not really correct: When the menopause event is added, 
  # the value of donations_in_five_years should change as well.
  # tdc2 <- tmerge(data1=tdc1, 
  #                data2=donations,
  #                id=sample, 
  #                donations_in_five_years=tdc(donation_age, donations_in_five_years, 0))
  # sex2 variable
  # menopauses1 <- data %>% select(sample, sex) %>% mutate(age = 0, sex2 = if_else(sex=="male", "male", "pre"))
  # menopauses2 <- data %>% select(sample, sex) %>% mutate(age = 50, sex2 = if_else(sex=="male", "male", "post"))
  # menopauses <- bind_rows(menopauses1, menopauses2)                                                       
  # tdc3 <- tmerge(data1=tdc2,
  #                data2=menopauses,
  #                id=sample,
  #                sex2=tdc(age, sex2))
  # tdc3
}
```


```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "tdc_train.rds")
if (recompute || ! file.exists(filename)) {
  tdc_train <- form_survival_data(train)
  saveRDS(tdc_train, filename)
} else {
  tdc_train <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "tdc_full.rds")
if (recompute || ! file.exists(filename)) {
  tdc_full <- form_survival_data(basic)
  saveRDS(tdc_full, filename)
} else {
  tdc_full <- readRDS(filename)
}
```

#### Fit on train data.

```{r}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_fit_5.rds", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_tdc_fit_5 <- coxph(f5_cox_tdc, tdc_train)
  saveRDS(cox_tdc_fit_5, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_tdc_fit_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
cox_tdc_fit_5
```



```{r Compute time-dependent covariate Cox model effect sizes}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_tdc_cis_5.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_tdc_cis_5 <- tidy(cox_tdc_fit_5, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_tdc_cis_5, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_tdc_cis_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot time-dependent covariate Cox model effect sizes}
g <- plot_cis(cox_tdc_cis_5, "Time-dependent Cox model 5 FinnGen") + 
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_tdc_cis_5.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

#### Fit on full data

Fit on full data.

```{r}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_full_5.rds", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_tdc_full_5 <- coxph(f5_cox_tdc, tdc_full)
  saveRDS(cox_tdc_full_5, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_tdc_full_5 <- readRDS(filename)
}
cox_tdc_full_5
```

```{r Compute time-dependent covariate full Cox model effect sizes}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_tdc_cis_full_5.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_tdc_cis_full_5 <- tidy(cox_tdc_full_5, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_tdc_cis_full_5, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_tdc_cis_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot time-dependent covariate full Cox model effect sizes}
g <- plot_cis(cox_tdc_cis_full_5, "Time-dependent full Cox model 5 FinnGen") + 
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_tdc_cis_full_5.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

Comparison with the old version.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- sprintf("%s/old_cox_tdc_cis_full_5.tsv", table_path)
old_cox_tdc_cis_full_5 <- read_tsv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE)
cox_comparison <- bind_rows(new = cox_tdc_cis_full_5, old = old_cox_tdc_cis_full_5, .id="model")
all_variables <- c(non_genetic_variables, genetic_variables)
g <- cox_comparison %>%
  mutate(term = fct_relevel(term, all_variables)) %>%
  plot_cis2("Cox model 5 full FinnGen")
  
g <- g + 
  labs(x="Hazard ratio", y="Variable") +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE, override.aes = list(shape = 16)))
g
```

## Model 6

Comparison model between FinnGen and UKBB.

### Basic logistic model

```{r}
variables6 <- c("sex", "age", #"donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                #"extra1"
                )
f6 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables6, collapse=" + "))
f6
```

```{r logistic-mod-6}
logistic_mod_6 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_mod_6
```

```{r}
logistic_recipe_6 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f6), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  # step_mutate(C282Y = as.integer(C282Y == 2),     # Encode C282Y and H1-2 as recessive snips
  #             H1_2  = as.integer(H1_2 == 2)) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
              #H1_2  = as.factor(H1_2)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(#starts_with("H1_2"), 
                                                   starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, 
                                                   starts_with("sex")  # donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
                                                   #rs3094093, rs114179634,
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
logistic_recipe_6
```


```{r logistic-workflow-6}
logistic_wflow_6 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_mod_6) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_recipe_6)
logistic_wflow_6
```

This will fit on training data and show performance measures on test data.

```{r last-fit-6}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_lf_6.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_lf_6 <- last_fit(logistic_wflow_6, basic_split)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_lf_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_lf_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(logistic_lf_6)
```

```{r full-fit-6}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_full_6.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_full_6 <- fit(logistic_wflow_6, basic)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_full_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_full_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
collect_metrics(logistic_lf_6)
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r logistic-vif-6}
logistic_lf_6 %>% extract_workflow() %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

Calibration.

```{r}
logistic_lf_6_test_pred <- augment(logistic_lf_6)
logistic_lf_6_test_pred %>%
  cal_plot_windowed(truth = hemochromatosis, estimate = .pred_case, step_size = 0.025, event_level="second")
```

#### Forest plots

```{r logistic-cis-6}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_6.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_6 <- compute_cis(logistic_lf_6 %>% extract_workflow())
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-plot-6}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_6.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_6, "Logistic model 6 FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_lf_6)) + labs(y="Variable")
#ggsave(file.path(figure_path, "poster", "logistic_cis_6.pdf"), g + theme(plot.title=element_blank(), axis.title.y.left = element_blank()))
if (save_figs) 
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-6}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_full_6.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_full_6 <- compute_cis(logistic_full_6)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_full_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_full_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r logistic-cis-full-plot-6}
#| fig-cap: The age is at first event for cases and at death or EOF for controls.
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_6.pdf")
g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_full_6, "Logistic model 6 full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_full_6)) + labs(y="Variable")
#g <- plot_cis(logistic_cis_full_6, "Logistic model 6 full FinnGen") + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs)
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
g
```


#### Risk table

Compute confidence intervals for predictions for multiple predictor combinations.

```{r}
# The value of age is stratified by sex
mean_ages6 <- train %>% group_by(sex) %>% summarise(age = mean(age))
data6 <- expand_grid(sex = c("male", "female"), H63D=0:2, S65C=0, C282Y=0:2, supersnip=0:2, 
                    rs3094093=2, rs114179634=0) %>% 
  mutate(across(sex, as.factor))
# data6 <- data6 %>% mutate(age = mean(train$age)) # This was a bad idea
data6 <- data6 %>% inner_join(mean_ages6, by="sex")
important_predictors6 <- c(Sex="sex", "H63D", "C282Y", HLA3="supersnip") %>% fill_names()

cat("The following variables were fixed during the computation of probabilities\n")
fixed_predictors6 <- get_fixed_predictors(data6, important_predictors6)
fixed_predictors6
```



```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
title <- "Model 6 train data"
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_6_train.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = train, col="sex", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = train, col="sex", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename, show_col_types = FALSE)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors6, title=title, col = "sex", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_6_train.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
```

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
title <- "Model 6 test data"
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_6_test.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = test, col="sex", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_lf_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = test, col="sex", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors6, title=title, col = "sex", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_6_test.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
  #plot(p, new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
#grid.newpage()
#png()
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
#dev.off()
```

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
title <- "Model 6 full data"
filename <- file.path(table_path, "risk_table_6_full.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  result  <- compute_risk_table(logistic_full_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = basic, col="sex", conf.level = 0.95)
  result2 <- compute_risk_table(logistic_full_6, data6, important_predictors6, count_data = basic, col="sex", conf.level = 0.80)
  result <- bind_rows(result, result2)
  write_tsv(result, filename)
} else {
  result <- read_tsv(filename)
}
p <- plot_risk_table(result, important_predictors6, title=title, col = "sex", conf.level = c(0.95, 0.80))
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "risk_table_6_full.pdf")
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
  #plot(p, new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
  plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
#plot(p, new_page=FALSE)
dev.new()
plot(p[[1]], new_page=TRUE)
plot(p[[2]], new_page=FALSE)
```

Combined effect.

```{r}
C282Y_plus_HLA3(logistic_cis_full_6)
```

### Alternative hemochromatosis definition

Dropped S65C because of collinearity.

```{r}
alternative_variables6 <- c("sex", "age", #"donations_in_five_years", 
                #"H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "S65C",
                "H63D", "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                #"extra1"
                )
alternative_f6 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(alternative_variables6, collapse=" + "))
alternative_f6
```

#### Logistic regression

```{r}
logistic_alternative_mod_6 <- parsnip::logistic_reg()
logistic_alternative_mod_6
```

```{r}
logistic_alternative_recipe_6 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(alternative_f6), data = train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  # step_mutate(C282Y = as.integer(C282Y == 2),     # Encode C282Y and H1-2 as recessive snips
  #             H1_2  = as.integer(H1_2 == 2)) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
              #H1_2  = as.factor(H1_2)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(#starts_with("H1_2"), 
                                                   starts_with("H63D"), supersnip,
                                                   S65C,
                                                   starts_with("sex")  # donations_in_five_years, sex2_pre, sex2_post
                                                   #rs3094093, rs114179634,
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
logistic_alternative_recipe_6
```


```{r}
logistic_alternative_wflow_6 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(logistic_alternative_mod_6) %>% 
  add_recipe(logistic_alternative_recipe_6)
logistic_alternative_wflow_6
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "logistic_alternative_full_6.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_alternative_full_6 <- fit(logistic_alternative_wflow_6, 
                                 basic2)  # Needs to be unfitted workflow
  saveRDS(logistic_alternative_full_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_alternative_full_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
s <- logistic_alternative_full_6 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% summary(correlation = TRUE)
s
```

Correlations between coefficients.

```{r}
s$correlation %>% as_tibble(rownames = "name1") %>% pivot_longer(cols=-name1, names_to="name2") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=name1, y=name2, fill=value)) + 
  geom_raster() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
rm(s)
```

Variance inflation factors.

```{r}
logistic_alternative_full_6 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% car::vif() %>% sort(decreasing = TRUE)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "logistic_cis_alternative_full_6.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  logistic_cis_alternative_full_6 <- compute_cis(logistic_alternative_full_6)
  write_tsv(logistic_cis_alternative_full_6, filename)
} else {
  logistic_cis_alternative_full_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
#filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_full_6.pdf")
g <- logistic_cis_alternative_full_6 %>%
  # Filter out variables whose confidence intervals were really large
  filter(! (conf.high > 1e20 | conf.low < 1e-20)) %>%
  plot_cis("Logistic model 6 alternative full FinnGen", subtitle=model_stats(logistic_alternative_full_6)) + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "logistic_cis_alternative_full_6.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```

#### Firth's logistic regression.

```{r}
baked <- logistic_alternative_full_6 %>% extract_recipe() %>% bake(new_data = basic2)
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "firth_alternative_full_6.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  firth_alternative_full_6 <- logistf(formula = hemochromatosis ~ ., data = baked)
  saveRDS(firth_alternative_full_6, filename)
} else {
  firth_alternative_full_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
```



```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "firth_cis_alternative_full_6.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  firth_cis_alternative_full_6 <- tidy_firth(firth_alternative_full_6)
  write_tsv(firth_cis_alternative_full_6, filename)
} else {
  firth_cis_alternative_full_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
g <- firth_cis_alternative_full_6 %>% 
  plot_cis("Firth model 6 alternative full FinnGen") + labs(y="Variable")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "firth_cis_alternative_full_6.pdf")
  ggsave(filename, g, width=7, height=7)
}
g
```

### Survival

```{r}
baseline_variables6 <- c("sex", "H63D", "S65C", 
                        "supersnip",
                        #credible_snip_names,
                        "rs3094093", "rs114179634",
                        "C282Y", 
                        "C282Y:(H63D + S65C + supersnip + sex)")
variables6_cox <- c(baseline_variables6)
f6_cox <- as.formula(sprintf("Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ %s", paste(variables6_cox, collapse=" + ")))
f6_cox
```

```{r}
cox_train <- train %>% mutate(hemochromatosis = as.integer(hemochromatosis == "case"),
                              across(c(C282Y, H63D), as.factor))
cox_full <- basic %>% mutate(hemochromatosis = as.integer(hemochromatosis == "case"),
                              across(c(C282Y, H63D), as.factor))
```

#### Kaplan-Meier curves

##### Adjust by C282Y

```{r}
km_fit <- survival::survfit(Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ C282Y, data=cox_full)
km_fit
```

https://stats.stackexchange.com/questions/402432/why-median-is-na-for-some-of-the-group-outcomes-in-survival-analysis

> If one of the groups has not yet dropped to 50% survival at the end of the available data, you cannot compute a median 
> survival and there will be NA values for median survival in such cases. Even if median survival has been reached in a group, > it might not be possible to calculate complete confidence intervals for those median values, as you have seen.

What is the survival at age of 90?

```{r}
tidy(km_fit) %>% filter(time >= 90, strata == "C282Y=2") %>% slice_min(order_by = time, n=1)
```

```{r}
#autoplot(km_fit) + labs(x="Age", y="Survival from anemia")
gsp <- survminer::ggsurvplot(km_fit, #palette = "#2E9FDF", 
                      conf.int = TRUE, 
           #fun = "cumhaz",   # cumulative hazards instead of survival
           fun = NULL,
           censor = FALSE,
           risk.table = TRUE, risk.table.y.text.col=TRUE, risk.table.height = 0.3)
```


```{r}
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "kaplan_meier_C282Y.pdf")
title <- "Kaplan-Meier C282Y"
pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
print(gsp, newpage=FALSE)
invisible(dev.off())
```


```{r}
#dev.new(noRStudioGD = TRUE)
grid.newpage()
print(gsp, newpage = FALSE)
#invisible(dev.off())
#gsp
```

##### Adjust by C282Y and sex

```{r}
km_fit2 <- survival::survfit(Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ C282Y + sex, data=cox_full)
km_fit2
```

```{r}
#tidy(km_fit2) %>% filter(time >= 90, strata == "C282Y=2") %>% slice_max(order_by = estimate, n=1)
km_df2 <- tidy(km_fit2) %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(cols = strata, delim=",", names=c("C282Y", "sex"))
filename <- file.path(table_path, "kaplan_meier_C282Y_sex.tsv.gz")
if (save_tables)
  write_tsv(km_df2 %>% select(-c(n.risk, n.event, n.censor)), filename)
km_df2 %>%  
  filter(time >= 90, C282Y == "C282Y=2") %>%
  group_by(sex) %>% slice_min(order_by = time, n = 1) %>% ungroup()
```


```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
gsp2 <- survminer::ggsurvplot(km_fit2, #palette = "#2E9FDF", 
                      conf.int = TRUE,
                      xlab = "Age",
           #fun = "cumhaz",   # cumulative hazards instead of survival
           fun = NULL,
           censor = FALSE,
           risk.table = TRUE, risk.table.y.text.col=TRUE, risk.table.height = 0.4)
grid.newpage()
print(gsp2, newpage = FALSE)
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(figure_path, "kaplan_meier_C282Y_sex.pdf")
title <- "Kaplan-Meier C282Y, sex"
pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
print(gsp2, newpage=FALSE)
invisible(dev.off())
```

##### Adjust by C282Y/H63D combination

```{r}
genotype_order <- c("C282Y homozygote", "Compound heterozygote", "H63D homozygote", "Non-carrier")
tmp <- cox_full %>% mutate(genotype = case_when(
  C282Y == 2 ~ "C282Y homozygote",
  H63D == 2  ~ "H63D homozygote",
  C282Y == 1 & H63D == 1 ~ "Compound heterozygote",
  TRUE ~ "Non-carrier"
))
```


```{r}
km_fit3 <- survival::survfit(Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ genotype, data=tmp)
print(km_fit3)
```


```{r}
km_df3 <- tidy(km_fit3) %>% 
  mutate(genotype = str_remove(strata, "genotype="))
  #separate_wider_delim(cols = strata, delim=",", names=c("C282Y", "sex"))
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "kaplan_meier_genotype.tsv.gz")
  write_tsv(km_df3 %>% select(-c(n.risk, n.event, n.censor)), filename)
}
# What is the survival at age 90?
km_df3 %>%  
  filter(time >= 90) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = time, n = 1, by = "genotype")
```


```{r}
gsp3 <- survminer::ggsurvplot(km_fit3, #palette = "#2E9FDF", 
                      conf.int = TRUE,
                      xlab = "Age",
           fun = NULL,
           censor = FALSE,
           legend.labs =  genotype_order,
           break.time.by = 30,
           risk.table = TRUE, risk.table.y.text.col=TRUE, risk.table.height = 0.4)
# Save plot to file
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "kaplan_meier_genotype.pdf")
  title <- "Kaplan-Meier genotype"
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
  print(gsp3, newpage=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
# Display plot
grid.newpage()
print(gsp3, newpage = FALSE)
```


##### Adjust by C282Y/H63D combination and sex


```{r}
km_fit4 <- survival::survfit(Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis) ~ genotype + sex, data=tmp)
print(km_fit4)
```


```{r}
km_df4 <- tidy(km_fit4) %>% 
  separate_wider_delim(cols = strata, delim=", ", names=c("genotype", "sex")) %>%
  mutate(genotype = str_remove(genotype, "genotype="),
         sex = str_trim(str_remove(sex, "sex="))) #%>%
  #select(genotype, sex)
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "kaplan_meier_genotype_sex.tsv.gz")
  write_tsv(km_df4 %>% select(-c(n.risk, n.event, n.censor)), filename)
}
# What is the survival at age 90?
km_df4 %>%  
  filter(time >= 90) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = time, n = 1, by = c("genotype", "sex"))
```


```{r}
genotype_order2 <- expand_grid(genotype = genotype_order, sex = c("male", "female")) %>% 
  glue_data("{genotype}, {sex}")
gsp4 <- survminer::ggsurvplot(km_fit4, #palette = "#2E9FDF", 
                      conf.int = TRUE,
                      xlab = "Age",
           fun = NULL,
           censor = FALSE,
           legend.labs =  genotype_order2,
           break.time.by = 30,
           risk.table = TRUE, risk.table.y.text.col=TRUE, risk.table.height = 0.4)
# Save plot to file
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- file.path(figure_path, "kaplan_meier_genotype_sex.pdf")
  title <- "Kaplan-Meier genotype, sex"
  pdf(filename, width=12, height=7, title=title)
  print(gsp4, newpage=FALSE)
  invisible(dev.off())
}
# Display plot
grid.newpage()
print(gsp4, newpage = FALSE)
```



#### Cox regression

```{r}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_fit_6.rds", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_fit_6 <- survival::coxph(f6_cox, data=cox_train)
  saveRDS(cox_fit_6, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_fit_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
summary(cox_fit_6)
```

```{r}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_full_6.fit", model_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_full_6 <- survival::coxph(f6_cox, data=cox_full)
  saveRDS(cox_full_6, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_full_6 <- readRDS(filename)
}
summary(cox_full_6)
```

Nothing interesting in this. This is a bit pointless, since the curve is for average of the data.

```{r}
g <- survminer::ggsurvplot(survfit(cox_fit_6), cox_train, risk.table = TRUE)
g
```


```{r Compute basic Cox model effect sizes}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_6.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_cis_6 <- tidy(cox_fit_6, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_cis_6, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_cis_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot basic Cox model effect sizes}
g <- plot_cis(cox_cis_6, "Cox model 6 FinnGen") +
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_6.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

```{r Compute basic full Cox model effect sizes}
filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_full_6.tsv", table_path)
if (recompute | !file.exists(filename)) {
  cox_cis_full_6 <- tidy(cox_full_6, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
    drop_na(estimate)    # Some interacting levels don't exist in the date, we drop them here
  write_tsv(cox_cis_full_6, file=filename)
} else {
  cox_cis_full_6 <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```


```{r Plot basic full Cox model effect sizes}
g <- plot_cis(cox_cis_full_6, "Cox model 6 full FinnGen") +
  labs(x="Hazard ratio") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/cox_cis_full_6.png", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="png")
}
g
```

#### Risk table

```{r}
data6_surv <- data6 %>% 
  mutate(across(c(sex, H63D, C282Y), as.factor)) # Convert to same column types as in the data used to fit the model
```

Predict survival curves for the individuals in the synthetic data.

```{r}
res <- survfit(cox_fit_6, newdata = data6_surv %>% head(n=10), conf.int = 0.95, 
        ctype = 2,   # Correct for ties
        censor = FALSE
        )
```

### Same using tidymodels

```{r}
baseline_variables6t <- c("sex", "H63D", "S65C", 
                        "supersnip",
                        "rs3094093", "rs114179634",
                        "C282Y")
variables6t_cox <- c(baseline_variables6t)
f6t_cox <- as.formula(sprintf("s ~ %s", paste(variables6t_cox, collapse=" + ")))
f6t_cox
```

```{r}
cox_mod_6 <- survival_reg() %>% 
  set_engine("survival") %>% 
  set_mode("censored regression")
```

```{r}
cox_train2 <- cox_train %>% mutate(s = Surv(event_age, hemochromatosis), .keep = "unused")
```

```{r}
cox_recipe_6 <- 
  recipe(as.formula(f6t_cox), data = cox_train2) %>% 
  #step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_mutate(C282Y = as.factor(C282Y),     # Encode C282Y, H63D and H1-2 as factors
              H63D  = as.factor(H63D)) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), C282Y, H63D) %>%
  step_interact(terms = ~ starts_with("C282Y") : c(
                                                   starts_with("H63D"), S65C, supersnip, 
                                                   starts_with("sex")
                                                   )) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
```

```{r}
cox_wflow_6 <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(cox_recipe_6) %>% 
  add_model(cox_mod_6)
```

```{r}
cox_fit2_6 <- cox_wflow_6 %>% fit(data = cox_train2)
cox_fit2_6
```

Get performance metrics in the train set and using cross-validation.

```{r}
set.seed(345)
cox_folds <- vfold_cv(cox_train2, v = number_of_folds)
cox_metrics <- metric_set(brier_survival_integrated, brier_survival,
                               roc_auc_survival, concordance_survival)
evaluation_time_points <- seq(20, 80, 20)
filename <- file.path(table_path, "cv_metrics.tsv")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  resamples <- cox_wflow_6 %>% 
    fit_resamples(cox_folds, metrics = cox_metrics, eval_time = evaluation_time_points, control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE))
  cv_metrics <- resamples %>% collect_metrics()
  rm(resamples)
  write_tsv(cv_metrics, filename)
} else {
  cv_metrics <- read_tsv(filename)
}
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(result_path, "train_preds.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  train_preds <- augment(cox_fit2_6, new_data=cox_train2, eval_time = evaluation_time_points)
  saveRDS(train_preds, filename)
} else {
  train_preds <- readRDS(filename)
}
train_metrics <- cox_metrics(train_preds, truth = s, estimate = .pred_time, .pred)
train_metrics
```

```{r}
both_metrics <- bind_rows(train=train_metrics, cv=cv_metrics %>% rename(.estimate = mean), .id="method") %>%
  select(-c(.estimator, n, .config))
```

```{r}
both_metrics %>% mutate(name = paste(.metric, coalesce(as.character(.eval_time), ""))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(.estimate, name, color = method)) + geom_point(position = position_dodge(width=0.3)) +
  lims(x=0:1)
```

The brier scores indicate an excellent calibration!

Let's show the ROC curves.

```{r}
tmp <- train_preds %>% roc_curve_survival(truth = s, .pred)
tmp %>% autoplot()
```

## Model 7

This includes all 67 Regenie predicted C282Y-interacting snips.

### XGboost


```{r}
variables7 <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years", 
                "H1_2", # is correlated with C282Y
                "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", 
                "supersnip",
                credible_snip_names,
                disease_names,
                interacting_snip_names,
                "rs3094093", "rs114179634"#, "rs9261354"  # HLA snips
                )
f7 <- sprintf("hemochromatosis ~ %s", paste(variables7, collapse=" + "))
f7
```

```{r}
# These hyperparameters should be optimised!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
xg_mod_7 <- boost_tree(learn_rate = 0.1, trees = 100) |>
  set_mode("classification") |> 
  set_engine("xgboost", verbose = 0)
```


```{r}
xg_recipe_7 <-
  recipe(as.formula(f7), data = xg_train) %>% 
  #step_relevel(hemochromatosis, ref_level = "control") %>%
  step_normalize(age) %>%
  step_integer(all_nominal_predictors(), all_of(disease_names), zero_based=TRUE) %>%
  step_lincomb(all_numeric_predictors()) # Removes variables that have exact linear combinations with others
xg_recipe_7
```

From
https://modeloriented.github.io/shapviz/articles/tidymodels.html
Remark: Dont use 1:m transforms such as One-Hot-Encodings. They are usually not necessary and make the workflow more complicated. If you cant avoid this, check the collapse argument in shapviz().

```{r xg-workflow-7}
xg_wflow_7 <- 
  workflow() %>% 
  add_model(xg_mod_7) %>% 
  add_recipe(xg_recipe_7)
xg_wflow_7
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(model_path, "xg_fit_7.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  tic("Fitting XGboost model")
  xg_fit_7 <- xg_wflow_7 %>% fit(xg_train)
  saveRDS(xg_fit_7, filename)
  toc()
} else {
  xg_fit_7 <- readRDS(filename)
}
class(xg_fit_7)
```

Took 73 seconds.

```{r}
xg_fit_7 %>% extract_recipe()
```

#### SHAP analysis

```{r}
set.seed(43)
xg_explain_7 <- xg_train %>% 
  #head(n=1000) %>% 
  slice_sample(n=10000) #%>%
  #mutate(hemochromatosis = fct_relevel(hemochromatosis, c("control", "case")))
X_pred_7 <- bake(  # goes to xgboost:::predict.xgb.Booster()
  xg_fit_7 %>% extract_recipe(), 
  has_role("predictor"),
  new_data = xg_explain_7,
  composition = "matrix"
)

#stopifnot(colnames(X_pred) %in% colnames(xg_explain_5))
```

Compute the SHAP values.

```{r}
# Note, the which_class parameter works only for multiclass classification, not for binary classification.
# shapviz assumes that the first level is the positive class. That cannot be changed, so we have
# to accept, that the directions of effects are opposite here.
filename <- file.path(result_path, "shap_values_7.rds")
if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
  tic("Computing shap values")
  set.seed(158)
  shap_values_7 <- xg_fit_7 %>% extract_fit_engine() %>%
    shapviz(X_pred = X_pred_7, X = xg_explain_7, which_class=1, interactions = TRUE) #|> 
  #setNames(levels(iris$Species))
  toc()
  saveRDS(shap_values_7, filename)
} else {
  shap_values_7 <- readRDS(filename)  
}
```



```{r}
shap_values_7 %>% 
  sv_importance() +   # By default this shows only top 15
  ggtitle("SHAP importance")
```

Store importances to file.

```{r}
xg_importance_7 <- shap_values_7 %>% sv_importance(kind="no") %>% enframe()
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "xg_importance_7.tsv")
  write_tsv(xg_importance_7, filename)
}
xg_importance_7
```

```{r}
shap_values_7 |> 
  sv_importance(kind = "bee") +
  ggtitle("SHAP importance")
```



```{r}
xvars <- c("sex2", "age", "donations_in_five_years" , "H63D", "S65C", "C282Y", "supersnip", "credible1", "credible2", "credible3", "rs3094093", "rs114179634", disease_names)
shap_values_7 |> 
  sv_dependence(xvars, color_var = NULL) +
  plot_annotation("SHAP importance")
  #plot_layout(ncol = 1, guides = "collect")
```

## Collect performances

### Incidence models

Resample the models.

```{r}
do_resampling <- function(workflow, folds, id, is_xg=FALSE) {
  filename <- file.path(model_path, glue("{id}.rds"))
  event_level <- if (is_xg) "first" else "second"
  metric_par  <- if (is_xg) mymetrics_rev else mymetrics
  if (! file.exists(filename) || recompute) {
    resamples <- 
      workflow %>% 
      fit_resamples(folds, metrics = metric_par, control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE, event_level=event_level))
    saveRDS(resamples, filename)
  } else {
```
