```{r}
    resamples <- readRDS(filename)
  }
  resamples
}
logistic_resamples_0 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_0, folds, "logistic_resamples_0")
logistic_resamples_1 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_1, folds, "logistic_resamples_1")
logistic_resamples_2 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_2, folds, "logistic_resamples_2")
logistic_resamples_3 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_3, folds, "logistic_resamples_3")
logistic_resamples_4 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_4, folds, "logistic_resamples_4")
logistic_resamples_5 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_5, folds, "logistic_resamples_5")
logistic_resamples_6 <- do_resampling(logistic_wflow_6, folds, "logistic_resamples_6")
xg_resamples_5       <- do_resampling(xg_wflow_5, xg_folds, "xg_resamples_5", is_xg = TRUE)
xg_resamples_7       <- do_resampling(xg_wflow_7, xg_folds, "xg_resamples_7", is_xg = TRUE)
```

Extract metrics.

```{r}
extract_metrics <- function(fit, data=train, xg_data=xg_train) {
  f <- extract_fit_engine(fit)
  if ("xgb.Booster" %in% class(f))
    res <- fit %>% 
      augment(new_data=xg_data) %>% mymetrics_rev(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class)
  else
    res <- fit %>% extract_workflow() %>% 
      augment(new_data=data)    %>% mymetrics(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class)
  res %>% select(-.estimator)
}
# Put all models and resampled data in a dataframe
models <- tribble(
  ~id, ~name, ~model, ~resamples,
  "logistic_lf_0", "Logistic 0", logistic_lf_0, logistic_resamples_0,
  "logistic_lf_1", "Logistic 1", logistic_lf_1, logistic_resamples_1,
  "logistic_lf_2", "Logistic 2", logistic_lf_2, logistic_resamples_2,
  "logistic_lf_3", "Logistic 3", logistic_lf_3, logistic_resamples_3,
  "logistic_lf_4", "Logistic 4", logistic_lf_4, logistic_resamples_4,
  "logistic_lf_5", "Logistic 5", logistic_lf_5, logistic_resamples_5,
  "logistic_lf_6", "Logistic 6", logistic_lf_6, logistic_resamples_6,
  "xg_fit_5", "XGBoost 5", xg_fit_5, xg_resamples_5,
  "xg_fit_7", "XGBoost 7", xg_fit_7, xg_resamples_7
)
#tmp <- list(logistic_lf_5 = logistic_resamples_5, xg_fit_5 = xg_resamples_5) %>% enframe("id", "resamples")
models <- models %>% mutate(train_performances = map(model, extract_metrics))
models <- models %>% mutate(test_performances = map(model, function(m) extract_metrics(m, test, xg_test)))
models <- models %>% mutate(cv_performances = map(resamples, ~ if (!is.null(.)) collect_metrics(.)))
train_performances <- models %>% select(id, name, train_performances) %>% unnest(train_performances)
test_performances <- models %>% select(id, name, test_performances) %>% unnest(test_performances)
cv_performances <- models %>% select(id, name, cv_performances) %>% unnest(cv_performances) %>% 
  mutate(.estimate = mean,
         low  = .estimate - 1.96 * std_err,    # Create 95% confidence intervals from standard error
         high = .estimate + 1.96 * std_err)
#bind_rows(xg=xg_perf1, logistic=logistic_perf1, .id = "model") %>% pivot_wider(names_from=model, values_from=.estimate) %>% select(-.estimator)
```

#### Areas under the curves

```{r}
method_order <- c("train", "cv", "test")
df <- 
  bind_rows(
    train=train_performances,
    test=test_performances,
    cv=cv_performances, .id = "method"
  ) %>% 
  filter(.metric %in% c("accuracy", "ppv", "roc_auc", "pr_auc", "brier_class")) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, method_order))
if (save_tables) {
  tsv_filename <- sprintf("%s/all_performances.tsv", table_path)
  write_tsv(df, tsv_filename)
}
g <- df %>%
  drop_na(.estimate) %>%
  mutate(across(c(name, method), fct_rev))  %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=.estimate, y=name, color=method, xmin=low, xmax=high)) + 
  geom_linerange(position = position_dodge(width=0.5), na.rm=TRUE) + 
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width=0.5)) + 
  scale_color_discrete(breaks = method_order) +
  facet_wrap(~.metric) +
  labs(x="Estimate", y="Model", color="Method", title="Performances", subtitle="With standard errors") +
  lims(x=0:1)
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/all_performances.pdf", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="pdf", title="All performances")
}
g
```

In some folds the ppvs are NA. This happens when the model predicts no individuals as hemochromatosis cases.

```{r}
models %>% pluck("resamples", 1) %>% collect_metrics(summarize=FALSE) %>% filter(.metric == "ppv")
```

```{r}
models %>% pluck("resamples", 1) %>% collect_predictions(summarize=FALSE) %>% mutate(id = as.factor(id)) %>% summary()
```

This tells more about problems with CV when there are so few cases. Maybe I should decrease the number of folds.


Testing:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
mymetrics1 <- metric_set(brier_class)
mymetrics2 <- metric_set(yardstick::metric_tweak("brier_class", brier_class, event_level = "second"))
mymetrics3 <- metric_set(yardstick::metric_tweak("brier_class", brier_class, event_level = "first"))
res <- logistic_lf_0 %>% extract_workflow() %>% 
      augment(new_data=train)
bind_rows(
  res %>% mymetrics1(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class),
  res %>% mymetrics2(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class),
  res %>% mymetrics3(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, estimate=.pred_class)
)
brier_class(res, hemochromatosis, .pred_case)
brier_class(res, hemochromatosis, .pred_control)
```

#### Curves 

Let's show the roc and pr curves for these models.

Extract predictions.

```{r}
helper_train <- function(id, m) extract_predictions(m, new_data = if (id %in% c("xg_fit_5", "xg_fit_7")) xg_train else train)
helper_test  <- function(id, m) extract_predictions(m, new_data = if (id %in% c("xg_fit_5", "xg_fit_7")) xg_test  else test)

models <- models %>% 
  mutate(cv_predictions = map(resamples, extract_predictions),
         train_predictions = map2(id, model, helper_train),
         test_predictions  = map2(id, model, helper_test),)

pred_cv <- models %>% select(id2=id, name, cv_predictions) %>% unnest(cv_predictions) %>%
  mutate(event_level = if_else(id2 %in% c("xg_fit_5", "xg_fit_7") , "first", "second"))
pred_train <- models %>% select(id2=id, name, train_predictions) %>% unnest(train_predictions) %>%
  mutate(event_level = if_else(id2 %in% c("xg_fit_5", "xg_fit_7"), "first", "second")) %>%
  select(c("id2", "name", ".pred_class", ".pred_control", ".pred_case",  "id", ".row", "hemochromatosis", ".config"))
pred_test  <- models %>% select(id2=id, name, test_predictions) %>% unnest(test_predictions) %>%
  mutate(event_level = if_else(id2 %in% c("xg_fit_5", "xg_fit_7"), "first", "second")) %>%
  select(c("id2", "name", ".pred_class", ".pred_control", ".pred_case",  "id", ".row", "hemochromatosis", ".config"))

event_level <- "second"
```

Compute cross-validation curves.

```{r}
roc_cv <- pred_cv %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  roc_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
pr_cv <- pred_cv %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

Compute train curves.

```{r}
roc_train <- pred_train %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  roc_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
pr_train <- pred_train %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

Compute test curves.

```{r}
roc_test <- pred_test %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  roc_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
pr_test <- pred_test %>%
  group_by(id2, name) %>% 
  pr_curve(hemochromatosis, .pred_case, event_level = event_level) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
roc_data <- bind_rows(cv=roc_cv, train=roc_train, test=roc_test, .id="method")
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "roc_data.tsv.gz")
  write_tsv(roc_data, filename)
}
g <- roc_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(1-specificity, sensitivity, group=name, color=name)) + geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~method) + labs(title="ROC")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/all_roc_curves.pdf", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="pdf")
}
g
```

```{r}
pr_data <- bind_rows(cv=pr_cv, train=pr_train, test=pr_test, .id="method")
if (save_tables) {
  filename <- file.path(table_path, "pr_data.tsv.gz")
  write_tsv(pr_data, filename)
}
g <- pr_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(recall, precision, group=name, color=name)) + geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~method) + labs(title="PR")
if (save_figs) {
  filename <- sprintf("%s/all_pr_curves.pdf", figure_path)
  ggsave(filename=filename,  plot=g, width = 180,  height = 120, units="mm", dpi=300, scale=1.0, device="pdf")
}
g
```

### Model formulas

```{r}
# The extra braces below are needed to make . work correctly in the pipeline. See
# https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42385010/using-the-pipe-and-dot-notation
models %>%
  head(n = -2) %>%   # Doesn't work for xgboost models
  {
  walk2(.$name, .$model, 
        function(name, model) {
          cat(name, ":\n", sep="")
          model %>% extract_fit_engine() %>% formula() %>% deparse1() %>% cat("\n")
        }
  )
  }
```

```{r}
cat("XGBoost 5:\n")
cat(f5, "\n")
cat("XGBoost 7:\n")
cat(f7)
```

## Extract information

```{r}
df <- read_tsv(file.path(model_path, "models.tsv"))
df <- df %>% 
  filter(type %in% c("cox", "logistic")) %>% 
  filter(filename != "cox_full_6.rds") %>% # What's wrong with this? It does not exist!
  #filter(type %in% c("xg", "rf")) %>%    # There's not much information in these
  mutate(model = map(filename, function(f) readRDS(file.path(result_path, "models", f))))
```

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "model_info.tsv")
if (recompute || ! file.exists(filename)) {
  model_info <- df %>% mutate(tmp = map(model, model_stats2)) %>% unnest(tmp) %>% select(-model)
  write_tsv(model_info, filename)
} else {
  model_info <- read_tsv(filename)
}
model_info
```

Since the train data was fitted on the split data with final_fit(), and full data was
fitted on full data with fit(), the returned object types are different. Hence, below I have
to first called extract_fit_engine() before glance.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "glance_logistic.tsv")
if (recompute || ! file.exists(filename)) {
  glance_logistic <- df %>% 
    filter(type == "logistic") %>% # head(2) %>% 
    mutate(tmp = map(model, function(f) glance(extract_fit_engine(f)))) %>% 
    unnest(tmp) %>%
    select(-model)
  write_tsv(glance_logistic, filename)
} else {
  glance_logistic <- read_tsv(filename)
}
glance_logistic
```

Because some Cox models are time-dependent covariate models, the n variable does not make much sense
for them.

```{r}
filename <- file.path(table_path, "glance_cox.tsv")
if (recompute || ! file.exists(filename)) {
  glance_cox <- df %>% filter(type == "cox") %>% mutate(tmp = map(model, glance)) %>% unnest(tmp) %>% select(-model)
  write_tsv(glance_cox, filename)
} else {
  glance_cox <- read_tsv(filename)
}
glance_cox
```



The concordance seems to be slightly better in the tdc model.
